summary: Categorical thermodynamic equilibrium: a configuration omega_eq on tau^3 with vanishing defect entropy S_def[omega_eq] = 0, meaning all holomorphic continuations are structurally trivial -- no defect bundles, no non-holomorphic insertions, no physically resolvable disorder.

name: Not the same as thermal equilibrium
summary: Categorical equilibrium (zero defect entropy, minimal disorder) differs fundamentally from classical thermal equilibrium (maximal total entropy, maximal disorder); the two notions disagree on the final state: a coherent vacuum versus a featureless thermal bath.

name: Defect absorption rate
summary: Defect absorption rate: at orbit depth n+1, the kernel condition reduces defect support by at least the gravitational self-coupling factor, |supp(d_{n+1})| <= (1 - iota_tau) |supp(d_n)| where (1 - iota_tau) approx 0.6585 = kappa(D;1).

name: The role of gravity in ordering
summary: Gravity is the primary ordering mechanism of the macroscopic universe: the defect absorption rate is controlled by the gravitational self-coupling kappa(D;1) = 1 - iota_tau, the largest self-coupling in the system, making the D-sector a net defect absorber.

name: Weak redistribution preserves defect count
summary: Weak redistribution preserves defect count: the A-sector (generator pi, coupling kappa(A;1) = iota_tau) permutes defect content among sub-cells at each refinement step without reducing total defect support -- it is measure-preserving on defect support.

name: Geometric contraction of defect support
summary: Geometric contraction lemma for defect support: if a_{n+1} <= (1 - iota_tau) a_n, then a_n <= (1 - iota_tau)^n a_0, the series sum_{n>=0} a_n <= a_0/iota_tau is finite, and a_n -> 0. The contraction factor is the gravitational coupling.

name: The contraction rate is the gravitational coupling
summary: The contraction rate (1 - iota_tau) approx 0.6585 is not a free parameter but the gravitational self-coupling kappa(D;1) derived in Book IV; the rate at which the universe orders itself equals the number determining G = (c^3/hbar) iota_tau^2. Gravity and thermodynamic ordering are twin readouts.

name: Defect support exhaustion
summary: Defect support exhaustion: starting from any initial configuration, defect support at depth n satisfies |supp(d_n)| <= (1 - iota_tau)^n |supp(d_0)|, and the total defect support summed over all depths is finite, bounded by |supp(d_0)|/iota_tau.

name: Coherence horizon
summary: Coherence horizon: the orbit depth n_coh = min{n in N : S_def(n) = 0} at which defect entropy first reaches zero; for n >= n_coh the configuration is in categorical equilibrium. Existence and finiteness guaranteed by the Global Defect Exhaustion Theorem.

name: Circulation, not stasis
summary: Categorical equilibrium is circulation, not stasis: the alpha-orbit continues beyond the coherence horizon, but evolution is defect-free -- boundary holonomy algebra circulates without dissipation or entropy production. Panta Rhei at the thermodynamic level: everything flows, even in equilibrium.

name: The 180^circ inversion
summary: The 180-degree inversion: classical Boltzmann entropy satisfies dS_class/dt >= 0 while defect entropy satisfies dS_def/dn <= 0 under the identification t <-> n (orbit depth). The two entropies have exactly opposite monotonicity.

name: Orbit steps versus physical time
summary: Orbit steps versus physical time: the defect exhaustion bound n_coh approx 661 orbit steps is small in the tau-native time parameter, but one orbit step may correspond to Planck-scale or cosmological-scale physical time depending on the calibration anchor regime.

name: Defect partition of paths
summary: Defect partition of paths: at orbit depth n, CR-compatible paths of length <= r from node n are classified as defect-traversing (passing through at least one defect site where |dbar_b f| > 0) or defect-free (avoiding all defect sites in D_n).

name: Defect entropy
summary: Defect entropy at orbit depth n: S_def(n) = lim_{r->inf} (1/r) ln(1 + P_def(n,r)), measuring the exponential growth rate of defect-traversing paths; the +1 inside the logarithm ensures S_def = 0 when the defect path count vanishes (vacuum).

name: Refinement entropy
summary: Refinement entropy at orbit depth n: S_ref(n) = lim_{r->inf} (1/r) ln P_free(n,r), counting the exponential growth rate of defect-free holomorphic paths; these paths exist even in the vacuum because the lattice itself provides defect-free continuations.

name: Entropy splitting
summary: Entropy splitting theorem: total holomorphic entropy decomposes as S(n) = S_def(n) + S_ref(n) + epsilon(n), where the cross-term epsilon(n) >= 0 satisfies epsilon(n) <= S_def(n); when S_def = 0, the total entropy equals the refinement entropy exactly.

name: Why varepsilon is harmless
summary: The cross-term epsilon is harmless: bounded above by S_def(n), it inherits monotone decrease and vanishes at the coherence horizon; the approximation S approx S_def + S_ref is exact to the precision required for all qualitative and most quantitative arguments.

name: Defect entropy is bounded
summary: Defect entropy is bounded: 0 <= S_def(n) <= S_def(0) for all n >= 0, where S_def(0) is the initial defect entropy. Defect entropy can never exceed its initial value and is always non-negative.

name: Defect entropy is monotonically decreasing
summary: Defect entropy is monotonically decreasing: S_def(n+1) <= (1 - iota_tau) S_def(n) for all orbit depths n >= 0; the contraction factor (1 - iota_tau) is the gravitational self-coupling, ensuring exponential decay of defect entropy.

name: Defect entropy reaches zero
summary: Defect entropy reaches zero: lim_{n->inf} S_def(n) = 0 with exponentially fast convergence S_def(n) <= (1 - iota_tau)^n S_def(0). The defect entropy is driven to zero by the geometric contraction at rate (1 - iota_tau) per orbit step.

name: Refinement entropy grows without bound
summary: Refinement entropy grows without bound: S_ref(n) >= n ln p + S_ref(0) where p is the refinement prime; in particular S_ref(n) -> infinity as n -> infinity, since each refinement step adds at least ln p new defect-free paths.

name: The paradox resolved
summary: The classical second-law paradox resolved: total entropy S increases because S_ref increases, but physical disorder S_def decreases. The classical observer measures the wrong component -- the readout functor projects onto total entropy, conflating refinement artifact with physical disorder.

name: Readout projects onto total entropy
summary: Readout projects onto total entropy: the readout functor R satisfies R(S_def + S_ref) = R(S_def) + R(S_ref), but the individual projections R(S_def) and R(S_ref) are not separately accessible to any E1 measurement.

name: The 99.99% that is noise
summary: At late orbit depths, the defect contribution S_def/S is exponentially small: virtually all entropy measured by an orthodox thermometer is refinement entropy -- a label count, not a disorder count. The classical second law accurately describes a quantity that is almost entirely physical noise.

name: iota_tau controls the splitting ratio
summary: iota_tau controls the splitting ratio: S_def(n)/S(n) <= (1 - iota_tau)^n S_def(0)/(n ln p); the crossover depth n* at which defect entropy drops below refinement entropy is determined by the master constant iota_tau.

name: Defect entropy from defect functional
summary: Defect entropy from defect functional: S_def(n) = ln|supp(delta[omega]_n)| + O((ln n)/n), relating the defect entropy at orbit depth n to the logarithm of the support of the 4-component defect functional D(d) = (mu, nu, kappa, theta).

name: The Penrose puzzle
summary: The Penrose puzzle (extraordinarily low gravitational entropy of the early universe) is natural in the tau-decomposition: the gravitational sector was maximally active at early times (absorbing defects at full rate), explaining the low initial gravitational entropy without the Past Hypothesis.

name: Finite initial defect count
summary: Finite initial defect count: at orbit depth n = 0, the number of defect sites |D_0| <= |Lambda_CR^(0)| < infinity, bounded by the finite lattice at the coarsest refinement level -- a quotient of Z^2 by T^2 periodicity, reduced modulo the coarsest prime power.

name: Contrast with QFT
summary: Contrast with QFT: in quantum field theory, the mode count is infinite (causing UV catastrophe, vacuum energy divergence, renormalization need); in the tau-framework, the mode count is finite at every level -- lattice Lambda_CR^(n) has p^{2n} points on T^2 and all sums converge.

name: Global defect budget
summary: Global defect budget: B_def = sum_{n=0}^{inf} |D_n|, the total defect support summed over all orbit depths, measuring the universe's total capacity for irreversible processes.

name: Finite defect budget
summary: Finite defect budget theorem: B_def <= |D_0|/iota_tau < infinity; the global defect budget is finite, bounded by the initial defect count divided by iota_tau. Follows from the geometric series bound in the defect support exhaustion corollary.

name: Integer threshold
summary: Integer threshold lemma: for a non-increasing sequence of non-negative integers satisfying a_{n+1} <= floor((1 - iota_tau) a_n), starting from a_0 = N, there exists finite n_0 <= N/iota_tau such that a_{n_0} = 0. Integer-valuedness forces exact termination.

name: Global Defect Exhaustion
summary: Global Defect Exhaustion Theorem: there exists a finite orbit depth n_coh < infinity such that |D_n| = 0 and S_def(n) = 0 for all n >= n_coh; the coherence horizon is bounded by n_coh <= ceil(ln|D_0| / ln(1/(1 - iota_tau))).

name: Coherence horizon---refined
summary: Coherence horizon (refined): n_coh = min{n in N : |D_n| = 0}, the smallest orbit depth at which the defect set is empty. By the Global Defect Exhaustion Theorem, this minimum exists and is finite; for p = 2 the bound gives n_coh <= 398.

name: Orbit steps are not years
summary: Orbit steps are not years: n_coh <= 441 is in orbit steps, not physical time units. The mapping depends on the calibration anchor; the finiteness of n_coh is regime-independent, but the physical duration may range from Planck-scale to cosmological-scale.

name: Finite irreversibility
summary: Finite irreversibility: every irreversible process (friction, dissipation, radioactive decay, entropy production) draws from the finite defect budget B_def <= |D_0|/iota_tau; after the coherence horizon, no further irreversible processes occur.

name: Contrast with heat death
summary: Contrast with heat death: classical heat death is an asymptotic limit that never exactly arrives (maximal disorder approached); the categorical coherence horizon is a finite event that definitely occurs, reaching zero disorder -- two fundamentally different endpoints.

name: Vacuum circulation is periodic
summary: Vacuum circulation is periodic: in the post-horizon regime (n >= n_coh), the evolution on the compact base tau^1 is periodic with period T_circ > 0; the alpha-orbit is holomorphic and defect-free, producing a rotation of the vacuum state around the temporal circle.

name: No Poincar'e recurrence conflict
summary: No Poincare recurrence conflict: in the tau-framework, Poincare recurrence occurs only in the post-horizon regime (coherent vacuum circulation), where S_def = 0 throughout; before the horizon, evolution is irreversible (defects absorbed), so recurrence never violates the Categorical Second Law.

name: Master exhaustion inequality
summary: Master exhaustion inequality: for initial defect count |D_0| = N: (i) |D_n| <= (1 - iota_tau)^n N, (ii) S_def(n) <= (1 - iota_tau)^n S_def(0), (iii) n_coh <= ceil(ln N / ln(1/(1 - iota_tau))). Consolidates all defect exhaustion results into a single three-part bound.

name: Defect half-life
summary: Defect half-life: n_{1/2} = ln 2 / (-ln(1 - iota_tau)) approx 1.66 orbit steps, the number of orbit steps for the defect count to halve. Controlled by the gravitational self-coupling; every approximately two orbit steps, half of remaining defects are absorbed.

name: Universal half-life
summary: Universal half-life: n_{1/2} approx 1.66 does not depend on defect type, sector, or physical regime -- all defects are absorbed at the same rate because the contraction factor (1 - iota_tau) is the D-sector self-coupling governing the temporal base direction uniformly.

name: The arrow has an endpoint
summary: The arrow of time has an endpoint: the arrow (the regime where S_def > 0 and irreversible processes occur) lasts for exactly n_coh orbit steps; beyond the coherence horizon, evolution is coherent circulation with no temporal asymmetry.

name: Time continues; the arrow does not
summary: Time continues but the arrow does not: time is the parameterization of the alpha-orbit (continues indefinitely on the compact group); the arrow of time is the direction of irreversibility (lasts n_coh steps). After the horizon, time flows but the arrow is absent.

name: The artificial trichotomy
summary: The artificial trichotomy: the classical radiation/conduction/convection classification is pedagogical convenience, not fundamental; all three are B-sector (electromagnetic) boundary exchange -- phonons are collective EM lattice modes, pressure gradients are electromagnetic.

name: EM energy transport
summary: EM energy transport: a change in the CR-tension distribution on tau^3 mediated by the B-sector of H_partial[omega], with energy Delta E_transport = integral over tau^3 of the pairing between the B-sector boundary holonomy variation and dbar_b f.

name: Radiation is B-sector transport
summary: Radiation is B-sector transport: the radiative energy flux from a defect configuration is j_rad = kappa(B;2) rho_def^2 c = iota_tau^2 rho_def^2 c, where the B-sector self-coupling iota_tau^2 controls the emission rate.

name: Conduction is near-field B-sector transport
summary: Conduction is near-field B-sector transport: thermal conduction in a lattice is mediated by near-field B-sector boundary characters with wavelength comparable to the lattice spacing; conductivity kappa_th = (1/3) c_V v_s l_mfp, where all three quantities are B-sector readouts.

name: Convective transport is B-sector displacement
summary: Convective transport is B-sector displacement: convective energy transport is the coherent displacement of the defect-functional profile driven by the B-sector pressure gradient, with flux q_conv = kappa(B;2) v dot nabla delta[omega].

name: alpha governs macroscopic energy transport
summary: Alpha governs macroscopic energy transport: the transport rate between any two macroscopic E1 configurations is proportional to the fine-structure constant alpha, as Gamma_transport propto alpha Delta E / Delta x^2; all three heat transfer modes scale linearly with alpha.

name: Why alpha and not iota_tau^2
summary: Why alpha and not iota_tau^2: the B-sector self-coupling kappa(B;2) = iota_tau^2 approx 0.1166 is the tau-native sector strength, while alpha approx 1/137 is its E1 readout after holonomy correction alpha approx (8/15) iota_tau^4. Macroscopic measurements see alpha because the readout functor includes the holonomy factor.

name: Geometric relaxation
summary: Geometric relaxation: the process by which a defect bundle loses CR-tension through spatial redistribution on the fiber T^2, driven by the fiber gradient of |dbar_b f|^2 weighted by the B-sector self-coupling kappa(B;2).

name: Topological relaxation
summary: Topological relaxation: the process by which a defect bundle is absorbed by the lemniscate boundary L = S^1 v S^1 through a change in topological sector, with energy release given by the variation of the boundary holonomy integral over L.

name: Hierarchy of relaxation times
summary: Hierarchy of relaxation times: geometric relaxation (spatial redistribution on T^2) is much faster than topological relaxation (absorption by lemniscate boundary), tau_geom << tau_top; the former occurs within fiber-scale interaction times, the latter requires many orbit steps on tau^1.

name: Heat is electromagnetism
summary: Heat is electromagnetism: all macroscopic energy transport at enrichment layer E1 is mediated by the B-sector of the boundary holonomy algebra, with heat flux Q-dot = integral over the boundary of the pairing between H_partial^B[omega] and delta[omega].

name: The tau-vacuum
summary: The tau-vacuum: the ground configuration omega_0 in H_partial[omega] satisfying dbar_b omega_0 = 0 everywhere (holomorphic throughout), S_def[omega_0] = 0 (zero defect entropy), and E[omega_0] = E_bdry (energy equals the boundary energy).

name: Vacuum energy is boundary energy
summary: Vacuum energy is boundary energy: E_vac = E_bdry = integral over lemniscate L of |H_partial[omega_0]|^2 d-sigma; the vacuum energy is a finite integral over the compact boundary L, determined by the master constant iota_tau via the Central Theorem.

name: Why no divergence
summary: No ultraviolet divergence: the QFT vacuum energy diverges because it sums infinitely many modes; the tau-vacuum energy is a single boundary integral over compact L -- no momentum integral, no UV divergence, no cutoff issue (Planck/string scale question does not arise).

name: QFT vacuum = refinement sum
summary: QFT vacuum equals refinement sum: the QFT vacuum energy density at cutoff level n corresponds to rho_vac^QFT(n) ~ p^{3n} hbar c / (2 l_ref); at the Planck cutoff, this gives the 10^{120} discrepancy. The sum counts lattice modes, not physical energy.

name: The vacuum catastrophe is a category error
summary: The vacuum catastrophe is a category error: the QFT vacuum energy density is not the energy of the vacuum state but a refinement count (lattice modes weighted by zero-point energy), corresponding to S_ref, not physical energy. The physical vacuum energy E_bdry is finite and cutoff-independent.

name: Vacuum source term is finite
summary: Vacuum source term is finite: T_vac = E_bdry/V = (1/V) integral over L of |H_partial[omega_0]|^2 d-sigma, finite and independent of any momentum cutoff; the 10^{120} discrepancy does not arise because no mode sum appears in the tau-Einstein equation.

name: Comparison with normal ordering
summary: Normal ordering comparison: QFT normal ordering :H: = H - E_0 removes the divergence without physical justification; the tau-framework explains why the subtraction is correct -- the zero-point contribution is refinement entropy, not physical energy. Normal ordering is accidentally correct for the wrong reason.

name: H_partial[omega
summary: The ground state of H_partial[omega] is the unique vacuum: satisfying S_def = 0 (zero defect entropy), E = E_bdry <= E[psi] for all configurations psi (minimal energy), dbar_b omega_0 = 0 on all of tau^3 (full coherence), and stability under perturbation.

name: Casimir effect from boundary modes
summary: Casimir effect from boundary modes: the Casimir force F_Cas = -pi^2 hbar c / (240 d^4) A is reproduced as the difference in boundary energies between constrained (plates) and unconstrained geometry, without invoking zero-point mode summation.

name: Casimir does not prove mode summation
summary: Casimir does not prove mode summation: the mode-sum and boundary derivations give the same numerical answer (standard QFT result); the Casimir effect does not distinguish between interpretations and is evidence for boundary energy, not for the reality of zero-point sums.

name: Data versus interpretation
summary: Data versus interpretation: the cosmic acceleration is observational data, but dark energy is a model-dependent interpretation from fitting H(z) to Lambda-CDM; the tau-framework preserves the data (acceleration is real) but replaces the interpretation (different cause).

name: Two faces of the same problem
summary: Two faces of the cosmological constant problem: (i) why so small? Lambda ~ 10^{-52} m^{-2} is 10^{120} times smaller than the Planck-scale value; (ii) why nonzero? A tiny nonzero value requires fine-tuning or a dynamical mechanism.

name: Defect-driven acceleration
summary: Defect-driven acceleration: as S_def decreases, the effective equation-of-state parameter w shifts from w > -1/3 (decelerating) to w < -1/3 (accelerating); the transition occurs when the defect-to-refinement ratio drops below 1/3.

name: Capacity surplus
summary: Capacity surplus: C_surplus(n) = C_total - |D_n|, the difference between the total absorption capacity of the lemniscate L and the current defect count; unused boundary capacity manifests as negative pressure in the effective equation of state.

name: No Lambda in the tau-Einstein equation
summary: No Lambda in the tau-Einstein equation: R^H = kappa_tau T contains no cosmological constant (Lambda = 0); the acceleration is a time-dependent phenomenon from the defect-to-refinement transition, not a constant vacuum energy term.

name: Dark energy is a readout artifact
summary: Dark energy is a readout artifact: the cosmic acceleration attributed to Lambda in Lambda-CDM arises from the defect-to-refinement transition on base tau^1; the orthodox readout functor misidentifies this as a constant energy density because it cannot decompose S = S_def + S_ref.

name: The 68% is refinement entropy
summary: The 68% is refinement entropy: the 68% of the cosmic energy budget attributed to dark energy in Lambda-CDM corresponds to the refinement entropy S_ref, which is a counting artifact (not physical energy); the tau-reading has 100% baryonic matter plus finite boundary energy.

name: Where does the 68% go?
summary: Where does the 68% go: it was never a real energy component; dark energy is not missing energy but misattributed refinement entropy. The tau-cosmic budget is 100% defect bundles on T^2 plus the finite boundary energy of the vacuum, producing the observed residual acceleration.

name: Testability
summary: Testability: w_eff(z) should vary with redshift -- w > -1/3 at high z (defect-dominated epoch), w approx -1 at low z (refinement-dominated); the transition redshift z_acc ~ 0.7 corresponds to S_def/S_ref = 1/3. Future surveys (DESI, Euclid, Roman) can test at percent-level.

name: III.T25 is enrichment-layer independent
summary: III.T25 (Positive Regularity Theorem) is enrichment-layer independent: its three structural conditions are preserved under the enrichment functor E0 -> E1 because enrichment is a faithful functor that does not create blow-up opportunities forbidden by the bare mathematical framework.

name: Macro defect-transport equation
summary: Macro defect-transport equation: D^macro_{n+1} = pr_base(Phi_{n,n+1}(d_n)), the base-projected evolution of the 4-component defect tuple (mu, nu, kappa, theta), where the fiber contributions from B, C, omega sectors are averaged and enter only through cross-couplings.

name: Fiber contributions are not discarded
summary: Fiber contributions are not discarded but averaged: base projection averages fiber contributions over T^2, keeping zero-mode and cross-coupling corrections -- the structural analogue of Reynolds averaging in orthodox fluid mechanics, smoothing microscopic fluctuations into effective viscosity and pressure.

name: Macro tau-Navier--Stokes flow
summary: Macro tau-Navier-Stokes flow: a sequence of tau-admissible configurations satisfying base-sector (D and A) dominance and macro viscous decay B^macro_{n+1} - B^macro_n proportional to the viscosity correction, defining the macroscopic fluid evolution on tau^3.

name: Compactness of tau^3
summary: Compactness of tau^3: the fibered product tau^3 = tau^1 x_f T^2 is compact in the profinite topology; every continuous function on tau^3 is bounded, every sequence of tau-admissible configurations has a convergent subsequence, and all defect-tuple components are bounded.

name: Macro three-condition sufficiency
summary: Macro three-condition sufficiency: the macro defect-transport equation satisfies conditions (C1) clopen locality, (C2) bounded extraction, and (C3) defect-horizon contractivity of the Positive Regularity Theorem III.T25 at the macroscopic scale.

name: Macro tau-NS regularity
summary: Macro tau-NS regularity: for every tau-admissible initial datum on tau^3, the macro tau-Navier-Stokes evolution produces a bounded velocity readout at every base point and fiber point; no macro-scale singularity forms. Follows from the three-condition sufficiency and compactness.

name: Contrast with mathbbR
summary: Contrast with R^3: on non-compact R^3, L^2-norm is not automatically bounded by L^inf-norm because volume is infinite -- energy can concentrate at a point without violating global L^2 bounds. On compact T^2 this is impossible; compactness is a structural feature, not a simplifying assumption.

name: No temporal blow-up
summary: No temporal blow-up: the macro tau-NS evolution on tau^3 = tau^1 x_f T^2 admits no temporal blow-up; the velocity readout is bounded at every point of the base tau^1. Follows from compactness of tau^3 and defect-horizon contractivity in the primorial (temporal) direction.

name: Macro tau-Reynolds number
summary: Macro tau-Reynolds number: Re_tau^macro = mu_n^macro / eta_tau^macro, the ratio of macro mobility to macro viscosity; determines whether the flow is laminar (Re << 1) or turbulent (Re >> 1).

name: The Reynolds number is bounded
summary: The Reynolds number is bounded above at every primorial level: Re_tau^macro <= M Prim(n)^{1/2} / eta_tau^macro, where the numerator grows as Prim(n)^{1/2} from the ABCD extraction bound. The Reynolds number can be large but never infinite.

name: Convective overshooting
summary: Convective overshooting (penetration of convective motions into the radiative zone) is a controlled boundary event in defect-tuple space -- a bounded violation of the convective-radiative inequality mu_conv > mu_rad at the interface, governed by the macro regularity theorem.

name: No singularity at the innermost stable orbit
summary: No singularity at the innermost stable orbit: in Category tau, the Schwarzschild radius is a boundary readout, not a singularity; the black hole interior is a defect-saturated boundary region. Accretion disk flow near the ISCO is turbulent but regular.

name: Honest claim
summary: Honest claim: the tau-framework does not solve the Clay Millennium NS Problem -- it solves a structural analogue (regularity on compact tau^3 for tau-admissible data). The domain gap (tau^3 vs R^3) and admissibility gap are genuine and have not been bridged.

name: Classical NS as readout
summary: Classical NS as readout: the classical incompressible Navier-Stokes equation on a chart domain U subset R^3 is the readout-functor image of the macro tau-NS equation on the corresponding region of tau^3, inheriting regularity on every compactly contained chart domain.

name: The chart domain is compact
summary: The chart domain is compact: U is the image of a clopen cylinder in tau^3 under the readout functor. The classical NS equation on a bounded domain with smooth boundary data is known to be regular (Leray's theorem); the tau-framework reproduces and extends this.

name: Limitations of K41
summary: Limitations of K41: the Kolmogorov self-similar prediction zeta_p = p/3 is violated for p >= 4 due to intermittency (rare intense events); correcting for intermittency (She-Leveque 1994) is a central open problem in turbulence theory.

name: tau-turbulent flow
summary: Tau-turbulent flow: a macro tau-NS flow with Re_tau^macro >> 1, non-monotonic defect budget B_n^macro across primorial levels n_inj <= n <= n_diss, and structured variation where budget decrease at each level is balanced by injection from the source. Turbulence is deterministic but structurally complex.

name: Macro energy spectrum
summary: Macro energy spectrum: in the tau-inertial range, E(k) = C_K epsilon^{2/3} k^{-5/3} (the Kolmogorov -5/3 law), where k is the wavenumber readout of the primorial level, epsilon is the budget flux, and C_K is determined by defect-tuple geometry.

name: The Kolmogorov constant C_K
summary: The Kolmogorov constant C_K approx 1.5-1.7 is determined by the defect-tuple geometry of the 4-component budget space (mu, nu, kappa, theta); precise computation requires the geometry of budget transfer on T^2, which is conjectural-scope.

name: tau-enstrophy
summary: Tau-enstrophy: Omega_n = (1/2)(nu_n^macro)^2, the squared vorticity component of the macro defect tuple at primorial level n; the structural analogue of classical enstrophy, governing vorticity budget evolution across the refinement tower.

name: Dual cascade decomposition
summary: Dual cascade decomposition in 2D: the defect budget decomposes into an inverse energy cascade (mu^2 transfers from high to low primorial levels) and a forward enstrophy cascade (nu^2 transfers from low to high levels); K5 sector isolation prevents mu-nu cross-transfer in the inertial range.

name: Batchelor--Kraichnan spectrum
summary: Batchelor-Kraichnan spectrum: the forward enstrophy cascade predicts E(k) propto eta^{2/3} k^{-3} in the enstrophy-cascade range, following from dimensional analysis applied to the vorticity budget flux eta = Omega_{n+1} - Omega_n = const.

name: Vortex stretching bound
summary: Vortex stretching bound: despite the amplifying nonlinearity mu dot nu, the vorticity component nu_n remains bounded at every primorial level: |nu_n| <= M_nu Prim(n)^{1/2}, where M_nu is determined by initial data. Compactness prevents blow-up.

name: She--L'ev^eque formula
summary: The She-Leveque formula zeta_p = p/9 + 2(1 - (2/3)^{p/3}) may be recovered from the hierarchical structure of the primorial tower, where the most intense structures correspond to configurations saturating the ABCD bound; rigorous derivation is open (conjectural scope).

name: Macro charge
summary: Macro charge: Q^macro(d) = integral over tau^1 of Hol_B(d|_{t x T^2}) dt, the base-integrated B-sector holonomy obstruction; the macroscopic charge is the total boundary obstruction accumulated over the temporal circle.

name: No Isolated Charges
summary: No Isolated Charges theorem: for any tau-admissible configuration on tau^3, the total boundary charge vanishes, Q_partial^total = integral over L of Hol_{B+C}(d) d-sigma = 0; every electric charge has a compensating partner, and global neutrality is a topological necessity.

name: Charge quantization
summary: Charge quantization is a theorem in Category tau: the holonomy takes values in a compact group, and compact-group representations are discrete (q in Z in natural units). In the orthodox framework, charge quantization is postulated; here it follows from the topology of L.

name: Sourceless macro flux
summary: Sourceless macro flux: for any closed surface Sigma in tau^3, the net B-sector flux vanishes, integral over Sigma of F_B dot d-Sigma = 0; every flux line entering a region must leave it. Gauss's law is trivially satisfied because every closed surface encloses zero net charge.

name: Local charges still exist
summary: Local charges still exist: the theorem does not preclude local charges (electron -1, proton +1), but tau-admissibility guarantees every local charge has a compensating defect somewhere on tau^3 so that total boundary holonomy vanishes.

name: No magnetic monopoles
summary: No magnetic monopoles: no tau-admissible configuration on tau^3 carries a net magnetic charge; integral over Sigma of B dot d-Sigma = 0 for every closed surface Sigma. The magnetic holonomy is trivial by the same boundary structure that forces electric neutrality.

name: Macro EM field
summary: Macro EM field: the chart-level readout of the B-sector defect components integrated over the base tau^1, F_mu-nu^macro(x) = R_mu(integral over tau^1 of D_B(t, x) dt), providing the macroscopic electromagnetic field tensor.

name: Macro color confinement
summary: Macro color confinement: no macroscopic configuration carries a net color charge; every hadron, nucleus, and astrophysical body is a color singlet. The C-sector contributes to the macro defect tuple only through confined composites (mesons, baryons) and cross-couplings kappa(A,C) and kappa(C,D).

name: Quark-gluon plasma is still confined
summary: Quark-gluon plasma is still confined in Category tau: the QGP is not deconfined but a state where the confinement scale is larger than the mean inter-quark distance. The global C-sector holonomy remains trivial; what changes is the characteristic confinement radius.

name: Macro current
summary: Macro current density: J^macro(x) = R_mu(q mu_B(x) v-hat_B(x)), the readout of the B-sector mobility flow, where q is the local charge, mu_B is the B-sector mobility, and v-hat_B is the unit velocity direction.

name: tau-plasma
summary: Tau-plasma: a macro defect configuration on tau^3 with nonzero B-sector defect density (n_B > 0), mobile charged carriers (mu_B > mu_crit), defining a structural plasma state through boundary obstruction density and carrier mobility conditions.

name: Forced Quasi-Neutrality
summary: Forced Quasi-Neutrality: in a tau-plasma, charge imbalance at any scale l > lambda_D is exponentially small: |n_+(l) - n_-(l)| <= n_0 exp(-l/lambda_D), where lambda_D is the Debye length. Quasi-neutrality follows from the B-sector boundary structure.

name: Plasma oscillations
summary: Plasma oscillations: a small charge perturbation in a tau-plasma oscillates at the plasma frequency omega_p = sqrt(n_0 e^2 / (m_e epsilon_0)); the oscillations are longitudinal (compression-rarefaction in electron density) and do not propagate below omega_p.

name: Plasma cutoff
summary: Plasma cutoff: the EM dispersion relation in a tau-plasma is omega^2 = omega_p^2 + c^2 k^2; for omega < omega_p the wave is evanescent (k^2 < 0), and for omega > omega_p it propagates with phase velocity v_phi > c and group velocity v_g < c.

name: Ionospheric reflection
summary: Ionospheric reflection: the Earth's ionosphere reflects radio waves below omega_p ~ 2pi x 10 MHz as a direct consequence of the B-sector cutoff -- electromagnetic modes below the plasma frequency cannot sustain propagating B-sector boundary obstructions.

name: Debye shielding
summary: Debye shielding: a test charge Q in a tau-plasma is screened with potential phi(r) = Q/(4pi epsilon_0 r) exp(-r/lambda_D); the Coulomb potential is exponentially suppressed beyond the Debye length lambda_D, following from boundary obstruction redistribution.

name: Debye number
summary: Debye number: N_D = n_0 lambda_D^3, the number of charge carriers in a Debye sphere. When N_D >> 1, collective effects dominate individual two-body collisions, characterizing the plasma as strongly collective.

name: No dark matter in the ICM
summary: No dark matter in the ICM: the five sectors exhaust the coupling budget (Sector Exhaustion Theorem); there is no dark sector. The missing mass in galaxy clusters will be accounted for by the modified D-sector gravitational readout.

name: MHD limit
summary: MHD limit: the magnetohydrodynamic regime of a tau-plasma where charge separation is below lambda_D, timescales exceed omega_p^{-1}, and spatial scales exceed lambda_D; the plasma is described as a single conducting fluid with frozen-flux constraint.

name: tau-MHD system
summary: Tau-MHD system: the coupled evolution of the macro tau-NS equation with Lorentz force source (J x B)/rho and the induction equation for B with Ohmic diffusion, defining the complete magnetohydrodynamic dynamics on tau^3.

name: Magnetic Reynolds number
summary: Magnetic Reynolds number: Rm = UL/eta_m = mu_0 sigma UL, where U is the characteristic velocity, L is the characteristic length, and eta_m is the magnetic diffusivity; determines whether flux is frozen in (Rm >> 1) or diffuses freely (Rm << 1).

name: Frozen-flux invariant
summary: Frozen-flux invariant (Alfven's theorem): in ideal tau-MHD (Rm -> infinity), the magnetic flux through any material surface is conserved, d/dt integral_S B dot dS = 0; field lines move with the fluid. Follows from B-sector holonomy preservation under ideal flow.

name: tau-reconnection event
summary: Tau-reconnection event: a localized, discrete change in the topological component theta_B of the B-sector defect tuple at a specific primorial level and fiber location where Rm_local drops below the critical value; the change in topological charge is discontinuous.

name: Sweet--Parker and Petschek
summary: Sweet-Parker (v_in ~ v_A/sqrt(Rm), too slow for solar flares) and Petschek (v_in ~ v_A/ln(Rm), fast but requires slow-mode shocks) reconnection models are compared; in Category tau, reconnection is a discrete topological event, not a continuous diffusion process.

name: Reconnection energy bound
summary: Reconnection energy bound: the energy released in a tau-reconnection event is bounded above by the magnetic energy difference Delta E_rec <= (1/2mu_0) integral_V (|B_init|^2 - |B_final|^2) dV between initial and final topological states.

name: Alfv'en wave dispersion
summary: Alfven wave dispersion: in a uniform tau-MHD equilibrium with background field B_0, small perturbations propagate as Alfven waves with omega = +/- v_A k_parallel, where v_A = B_0/sqrt(mu_0 rho) is the Alfven speed.

name: Magnetosonic dispersion
summary: Magnetosonic dispersion: fast and slow magnetosonic modes have omega^2 = (1/2) k^2 [(c_s^2 + v_A^2) +/- sqrt((c_s^2 + v_A^2)^2 - 4 c_s^2 v_A^2 cos^2 theta)], the two compressible MHD wave modes coupling sound speed c_s and Alfven speed v_A.

name: MHD instability condition
summary: MHD instability condition: an MHD equilibrium d_0 is unstable if there exists a perturbation delta-d such that the defect functional decreases, delta[omega](d_0 + delta-d) < delta[omega](d_0); the system evolves toward the lower-energy state.

name: Mixed-sector mode
summary: Mixed-sector mode: a propagating perturbation on tau^3 involving coherent oscillation between two or more sector components of the boundary holonomy algebra; the Alfven mode is the canonical example, mixing sectors B (electromagnetic) and D (gravitational).

name: Alfv'en speed as cross-coupling readout
summary: Alfven speed as cross-coupling readout: v_A^2 propto kappa(B,D) / (rho/B_0^2) = iota_tau^2 (1 - iota_tau) times the field-to-density ratio; the Alfven speed is a readout of the B-D cross-coupling strength, not an independent parameter.

name: The photon-in-fluid interpretation
summary: Photon-in-fluid interpretation: an Alfven wave is a photon dressed by matter, propagating at v_A = c (B_0/sqrt(mu_0 rho c^2)) instead of c; the dressing factor measures the relative weight of B-sector to D-sector contributions in the defect-tuple observable.

name: Alfv'en orbit
summary: Alfven orbit on tau^3: a trajectory gamma_A(t) in H_partial[omega] where the B-component oscillates as Hol_B = A cos(omega_A t + phi), the D-component oscillates in antiphase scaled by v_A/c, and the frequency satisfies the Alfven dispersion relation.

name: Sufficient energy flux
summary: Sufficient energy flux for coronal heating: the Alfven wave energy flux ~ 3 x 10^5 erg/cm^2/s matches the coronal energy loss rate; the tau-framework identifies the structural mechanism (mixed-sector oscillation and dissipation) converting photospheric convective energy to coronal thermal energy.

name: ISM Alfv'en cascade
summary: ISM Alfven cascade: in the interstellar medium, Alfven turbulence follows Goldreich-Sridhar scaling E(k_perp) propto k_perp^{-5/3} with critical balance k_parallel propto k_perp^{2/3}, a magneto-turbulent cascade where both velocity and magnetic field participate.

name: Crossing preservation
summary: Crossing preservation: the base projection pr_base preserves inequality crossings -- if the fiber-level defect tuple D crosses an inequality boundary, then the macro defect tuple D^macro = pr_base(D) also crosses the corresponding base-projected boundary.

name: Macro tau-crystal
summary: Macro tau-crystal: a macro configuration with mu^macro = 0 (rigid), nu^macro = 0 (no vorticity), large kappa^macro (high compression rigidity), and theta^macro nonzero (long-range topological order, i.e., crystalline lattice structure).

name: Macro tau-glass
summary: Macro tau-glass: a macro configuration with mu^macro = 0 (rigid), small but nonzero nu^macro (frozen residual vorticity), large kappa^macro (high compression rigidity), and theta^macro = 0 (no long-range topological order); distinguished from crystal by absence of theta.

name: The glass transition is not a phase transition
summary: The glass transition is not a phase transition in either framework: in the tau-framework it is an inequality crossing mu -> 0 at fixed theta = 0 -- a kinetic arrest in defect-tuple space, not a thermodynamic phase boundary with latent heat or entropy discontinuity.

name: First-order macro transition
summary: First-order macro transition: a discontinuity in the macro defect tuple at base point t_0, where lim_{t->t_0^-} D^macro(t) != lim_{t->t_0^+} D^macro(t) in one or more of the four components (mu, nu, kappa, theta), representing a sharp phase boundary.

name: Second-order macro transition
summary: Second-order macro transition: at base point t_c, the macro defect tuple D^macro(t) is continuous but one or more of its derivatives dD^macro/dt are discontinuous, and a symmetry of the defect-tuple configuration is broken or restored at t_c.

name: Critical surface is codimension 1
summary: Critical surface is codimension 1: each inequality function f_i (fluid/solid, ordered/disordered, compressible/incompressible) defines a codimension-1 surface in defect-tuple space; triple points are codimension-2 intersections, critical points are codimension-2 tangencies (structural Gibbs phase rule).

name: Gaia and crystallization
summary: Gaia and white dwarf crystallization: the Gaia-detected pile-up in the white dwarf luminosity function at the crystallization temperature (Tremblay et al. 2019) is consistent with the tau-inequality crossing at the mu = 0 boundary, where ions freeze and theta_lattice appears.

name: Neutron star phase sequence
summary: Neutron star phase sequence: a one-parameter family of inequality crossings parameterized by density rho: crystal -> drip (mu_n > 0) -> pasta (theta varies) -> core (kappa drops); each arrow is an inequality crossing in defect-tuple space.

name: DDT and turbulence
summary: DDT (deflagration-to-detonation transition) in supernovae is an inequality crossing in defect-tuple space: turbulent cascade transfers mobility budget from large to small scales, and when small-scale mobility exceeds the compression threshold, the front transitions to detonation.

name: Phase-transition universality
summary: Phase-transition universality: every macro phase transition on tau^3 is the base-projected image of a fiber-level inequality crossing in defect-tuple space; the fiber-level classification is the universal template, and macro phases are its readouts at specific density, temperature, and field conditions.

name: Universality is structural, not accidental
summary: Universality is structural, not accidental: critical exponents are independent of microscopic details because the critical surface is determined by defect-functional geometry depending only on axioms K0-K6; different materials are different initial conditions, but the critical surface is universal.

name: Newtonian Limit --- V.T30
summary: The tau-Einstein equation under weak-field, slow-motion, and negligible capacity-gradient conditions reduces to the Poisson equation with G = (c^3/hbar) iota_tau^2, recovering the Newtonian inverse-square law F = -GmM r-hat / r^2.

name: The three conditions quantified
summary: Quantifies the three Newtonian-limit conditions for the solar system: weak field |epsilon h| ~ 2e-6 at the Sun's surface, slow motion v/c < 2e-4 (Mercury), and negligible capacity gradient at all planetary distances.

name: Capacity Gradient as Apparent Dark Matter --- V.P20
summary: When the capacity-gradient condition (iii) fails at galactic scales, gravitational acceleration receives an additive correction g_cap(r) from the D-sector capacity gradient, reproducing apparent dark matter effects without new particles.

name: Classical Validity Scale --- V.D50
summary: The classical validity scale ell_cl is the radius at which r |nabla C_D|/C_D >= 1, marking the transition from Newtonian inverse-square behavior to the galactic regime where capacity gradients dominate.

name: The MOND scale as proxy
summary: Milgrom's MOND acceleration scale a_0 ~ 1.2e-10 m/s^2 is not fundamental but a proxy for the classical validity scale ell_cl: a_0 is the Newtonian acceleration at ell_cl for a typical galaxy.

name: Two-Regime Readout --- V.T31
summary: The gravitational readout functor exhibits two regimes: classical (r << ell_cl) with standard Newtonian g = GM/r^2, and galactic (r >> ell_cl) where the capacity gradient correction dominates, governed by the ratio r/ell_cl.

name: No free parameters
summary: The D-sector capacity profile C_D(r) is not a fitting function with adjustable parameters but is determined by the tau-Einstein equation solution for given boundary conditions, with coupling kappa_tau = 1 - iota_tau universal across all galaxies.

name: Bertrand as Readout Constraint --- V.P21
summary: Bertrand's theorem (closed orbits only for r^{-2} and linear forces) is a property of the classical readout regime within ell_cl; beyond ell_cl the capacity correction breaks Bertrand's hypotheses and orbits need not close.

name: Newton's First Law as Limit --- V.P22
summary: Newton's first law (straight-line motion of free bodies) is derived as a limit property of the readout: when all three Newtonian-limit conditions hold and the energy-momentum character vanishes, geodesics of g_partial[chi] become straight lines.

name: Correspondence Tower --- V.T32
summary: Each level of the gravitational approximation hierarchy (linearized, post-Newtonian, Newtonian, free-particle) is a systematic truncation of the full tau-Einstein equation, with the full equation fundamental and lower levels discarding information.

name: The dark matter debate is a projection artifact
summary: The dark matter debate is a projection artifact: the capacity gradient in the tau-Einstein equation already accounts for observed galactic dynamics without modifying the matter content or force law; dark matter searches seek a nonexistent particle.

name: Angular Momentum Character --- V.D51
summary: The angular momentum character L_chi(n) of a D-sector boundary state chi is defined as the integral of chi wedge d theta_pi over the level-n fiber of tau^1, encoding angular momentum as a boundary holonomy property rather than a manifold symmetry.

name: Rotational Flux Conservation --- V.T33
summary: For a D-sector boundary state evolving under the tau-Einstein equation in the weak-field, slow-motion regime, the angular momentum character is conserved along the refinement tower: dL_chi/dn = 0.

name: Kepler's First Law --- V.T34
summary: In the classical regime (all three Newtonian-limit conditions satisfied), every bound orbit of a test particle around a central mass M is a conic section; for negative total energy, the orbit is an ellipse r(theta) = a(1-e^2)/(1+e cos theta).

name: Kepler's Second Law --- V.T35
summary: Kepler's second law: the line joining a planet to the central mass sweeps equal areas in equal times, dA/dt = L/(2m) = const, following directly from the rotational flux conservation theorem.

name: Second law is deeper than the first
summary: Kepler's second law is deeper than the first: it requires only angular momentum conservation (any central force), while the first law additionally needs the inverse-square force. The second law remains approximately valid even beyond ell_cl.

name: Kepler's Third Law --- V.T36
summary: For bound orbits in the classical regime, the orbital period squared is proportional to the semi-major axis cubed: P^2 = (4 pi^2 / GM) a^3, with G = (c^3/hbar) iota_tau^2.

name: Kepler as theorem, not phenomenology
summary: Kepler's laws are theorems of Category tau derived from rotational flux conservation and the Newtonian limit of the tau-Einstein equation, not empirical fits or postulates. They follow from the structural properties of H_partial[omega].

name: Perihelion Advance from tau-GR --- V.P23
summary: Mercury's perihelion advance is computed from the linearized tau-Einstein equation with G = (c^3/hbar) iota_tau^2 to be 42.98 arcsec/century, agreeing with the observed 43.0 +/- 0.5 arcsec/century with no free parameters beyond iota_tau and m_n.

name: Light Deflection from tau-GR --- V.P24
summary: Light deflection by the Sun, delta theta = 4GM_sun/(c^2 R_sun) ~ 1.75 arcsec, follows from the linearized tau-Einstein equation applied to a null geodesic (gamma-sector null intertwiner); the factor 4 arises from coupling to both temporal and spatial perturbation components.

name: Shapiro Delay from tau-GR --- V.P25
summary: The Shapiro time delay Delta t = (4GM/c^3) ln(4 r_1 r_2 / d^2) follows from the temporal boundary-character perturbation in the linearized tau-Einstein equation, confirmed by the Cassini mission to 2e-5 relative accuracy.

name: All three tests pass with zero fitting
summary: Perihelion advance, light deflection, and Shapiro delay are all computed from the same tau-Einstein equation with coupling kappa_tau = 1 - iota_tau and G = (c^3/hbar) iota_tau^2, with no case-by-case fitting.

name: Lensing Character --- V.D52
summary: The lensing character chi_lens(n) maps incoming null intertwiner directions to outgoing directions via a boundary character on S^1_in -> S^1_out, encoding gravitational lensing as a D-sector projection through the gamma-sector null structure.

name: Heliophysics as readout of H_partial[omega
summary: Heliophysics (sunspots, flares, coronal heating, solar wind, heliosphere) is a readout of the interplay between D-sector gravitational rotation and B-sector electromagnetic boundary characters, with cross-coupling kappa(B,D) governing all solar magnetic phenomena.

name: Solar System Concordance --- V.P26
summary: The tau-Einstein equation with kappa_tau = 1 - iota_tau and G = (c^3/hbar) iota_tau^2 reproduces all solar system precision tests: Kepler's laws, Mercury's perihelion (42.98 arcsec/century), light deflection (1.75 arcsec), and Shapiro delay.

name: Galaxy as Relational Coherence --- V.D53
summary: A galaxy is defined as a connected region of H_partial[omega] with D-sector capacity exceeding a coherence threshold C_min, cross-sector excitation from B and C sectors, and finite spatial extent -- a relational coherence, not a collection of gravitationally bound particles.

name: No void between galaxies
summary: Between galaxies the boundary holonomy algebra remains active (D-sector nonzero, B-sector carries CMB, C-sector carries cosmic neutrino background); the intergalactic medium differs from a galaxy by sub-threshold coherence C_D < C_min, not by emptiness.

name: Modified Jeans Scale --- V.P27
summary: The tau-native Jeans wavelength lambda_J^tau = c_s sqrt(pi/(G rho)) (1 + r/(2 ell_cl))^{-1/2} is shorter than the classical Jeans wavelength when r ~ ell_cl, enhancing gravitational collapse without requiring dark matter.

name: Galaxy Formation Sequence --- V.P28
summary: Galaxy formation in Category tau proceeds through five conjectural stages: boundary seeding from CMB perturbations, capacity amplification during expansion, Jeans collapse at reduced tau-native Jeans scale, angular momentum capture via rotational flux conservation, and disk/bulge formation.

name: Conjectural scope
summary: The galaxy formation sequence is structurally grounded (capacity gradient, Jeans instability, rotational flux conservation) but quantitative details remain conjectural pending explicit solutions of the tau-Einstein equation with realistic boundary conditions.

name: Cosmic Web as Capacity Skeleton --- V.D54
summary: The capacity skeleton is the spatial pattern of D-sector capacity C_D(x) at a given orbit depth: filaments are ridges, nodes are local maxima (galaxy clusters), voids are local minima, walls are saddle surfaces. The cosmic web is its readout.

name: Web Determines Galaxy Locations --- V.P29
summary: Galaxy formation locations are determined by the capacity skeleton: galaxies form at or near local maxima of C_D, with the ~100 Mpc characteristic web scale set by the BAO scale encoded in the CMB constraint surface.

name: Filaments are not gravitational wakes
summary: Cosmic filaments are ridges of the capacity C_D connecting nodes, not gravitational wakes of dark matter. Matter accumulates along filaments because g_cap points toward the ridges.

name: Morphological Capacity Profile --- V.D55
summary: The morphological capacity profile classifies galaxies by the spatial shape of C_D(x): elliptical galaxies have slowly varying ellipsoidal profiles; spiral galaxies have disk-like profiles with non-axisymmetric perturbations producing spiral arms via density waves.

name: Morphology is not destiny
summary: Galaxy morphology evolves as the capacity profile evolves under the tau-Einstein equation with time-dependent boundary conditions: mergers can transform spirals into ellipticals, and gas re-acquisition can regenerate disks.

name: Galactic Virial Theorem --- V.P30
summary: A galaxy in relational coherence satisfies the tau-native virial theorem 2 K_eff + W_eff = 0, where K_eff includes the capacity gradient correction and W_eff is the effective potential energy.

name: Satellite planes and the missing satellite problem
summary: The missing satellite problem does not arise in the tau-framework: there are no dark matter subhalos, and satellite galaxies form from sub-threshold capacity peaks in the cosmic web, their number determined by the capacity skeleton.

name: Galactic Capacity Profile --- V.D56
summary: For a disk galaxy with baryonic surface density Sigma_b(r), the galactic capacity profile is the solution of nabla^2 ln C_D = -(4 pi G/c^2) Sigma_b - (1/ell_tau^2) ln(C_D/C_D^infty), a nonlinear equation with tau-scale length ell_tau.

name: Flat Rotation Curve Theorem --- V.T37
summary: For a disk galaxy with exponential surface density, the rotation velocity v_c(r) -> v_infty = (G M_b c^2 / (2 ell_tau))^{1/4} at large r, producing asymptotically flat rotation curves from the capacity gradient without dark matter halos.

name: Baryonic Tully--Fisher from Capacity --- V.R20
summary: The baryonic Tully-Fisher relation M_b = A v_infty^4 follows directly from the flat rotation curve theorem, with normalization A determined by G, c, and ell_tau (all functions of iota_tau and H_0), involving no dark matter parameter.

name: Galaxy-by-Galaxy Concordance --- V.P31
summary: The capacity mechanism predicts rotation curves in qualitative agreement with observations: high-surface-brightness spirals flatten at 2-3 R_d, low-surface-brightness galaxies show rising curves throughout. Quantitative fits require numerical solution of the capacity equation.

name: What ``conjectural'' means here
summary: The capacity gradient mechanism is tau-effective (follows from the tau-Einstein equation), but quantitative galaxy-by-galaxy rotation curve fits are conjectural because the capacity equation has not yet been solved numerically for individual galaxies.

name: MOND as Capacity Proxy --- V.P32
summary: The MOND interpolation function mu is an effective description of the classical-to-galactic regime transition, with acceleration scale a_0 ~ c^2/(2 ell_tau) serving as a proxy for the inverse tau-scale length.

name: MOND as approximation theorem
summary: MOND is an approximation theorem in the tau-framework: the capacity gradient correction in the regime g < a_0 can be well approximated by the MOND interpolation with universal scale a_0, analogous to post-Newtonian gravity working in the solar system.

name: No Dark Particle Theorem --- V.P33
summary: No persistent defect bundle on T^2 has dark matter properties (gravitational coupling + EM transparency): D-sector coupling is a base readout property, not a fiber defect bundle property, so all particles carry electromagnetic charge.

name: The asymmetry of falsification
summary: The dark matter paradigm adjusts halo parameters galaxy by galaxy (concentration, virial mass, inner slope). The capacity mechanism has no such freedom: the profile is fixed by the observed baryonic distribution and two universal constants (iota_tau, H_0).

name: RAR from Capacity Gradient --- V.P34
summary: The radial acceleration relation g_obs = g_N / (1 - exp(-sqrt(g_N/a_0))) follows from the capacity gradient mechanism: g_obs = g_N + g_cap, with tight scatter (~0.13 dex) reflecting the universality of the capacity equation across 153 galaxies.

name: Degeneracy Pressure Character --- V.D57
summary: The degeneracy pressure character P_deg = ((3 pi^2)^{2/3} hbar^2 / (5 m_e)) n_e^{5/3} is the B-sector boundary character encoding the resistance of a fully occupied Fermi sea to compression, with m_e from the tau mass ratio formula.

name: Chandrasekhar Mass from tau-Framework --- V.T38
summary: The Chandrasekhar mass M_Ch = omega_3^0 (hbar c / G)^{3/2} / (mu_e m_u)^2 ~ 1.44 M_sun is derived from the tau-framework by substituting G = (c^3/hbar) iota_tau^2, marking the B-sector electron degeneracy threshold against D-sector gravitational collapse.

name: White dwarf mass--radius relation
summary: White dwarfs have an inverted mass-radius relation R ~ M^{-1/3}: more massive white dwarfs are smaller, with R -> 0 as M -> M_Ch, signifying the relational threshold where the D-sector overwhelms B-sector electron degeneracy pressure.

name: Neutron Star EOS Structure --- V.P35
summary: The neutron star equation of state exhibits three regimes: crust (rho < rho_0, lattice with degenerate electrons), outer core (rho_0 to 3 rho_0, neutron-proton fluid dominated by C-sector), and inner core (rho > 3 rho_0, uncertain high-density strong-sector physics).

name: TOV Mass Limit --- V.T39
summary: For any equation of state satisfying causality (c_s <= c) and thermodynamic stability (dP/d rho >= 0), there exists a maximum neutron star mass M_TOV ~ 2.0-2.3 M_sun above which no static TOV solution exists.

name: The mass gap
summary: The lower mass gap between M_Ch ~ 1.4 M_sun and ~2.5 M_sun is not structural in the tau-framework: the transition from neutron star to black hole is smooth in EOS parameter space, consistent with LIGO-Virgo observations (GW190814) of objects in this range.

name: Black Hole as Maximal Topological Defect --- V.D58
summary: A black hole in Category tau is a region of tau^3 where the topological defect component saturates: d_top(x) = 1 for all x in the region. The event horizon partial B is the surface where d_top transitions from <1 to 1.

name: No primordial black holes
summary: Primordial black holes do not form in the tau-framework: black hole formation requires d_top = 1 from sufficient mass concentration, achieved only after stellar evolution produces compact cores; the early universe has d_top << 1 everywhere.

name: Compact-Object Classification --- V.T40
summary: Every compact stellar remnant is classified by core mass relative to two relational thresholds: M_c < M_Ch gives white dwarf (B-sector electron degeneracy), M_Ch <= M_c < M_TOV gives neutron star (C-sector nuclear pressure), M_c >= M_TOV gives black hole (d_top = 1).

name: Hulse--Taylor Agreement --- V.P36
summary: The Hulse-Taylor binary PSR B1913+16 orbital period derivative P_dot = (-2.423 +/- 0.001)e-12 agrees with the tau-Einstein prediction P_dot = -2.4025e-12 to 0.3%, within systematic uncertainty of the Galactic potential model.

name: Pulsars probe the strong-field regime
summary: Binary pulsars probe the strong-field regime (|epsilon h| ~ 0.2 at neutron star surfaces), where all three Newtonian-limit conditions fail. The Hulse-Taylor agreement is therefore a strong-field test of the tau-Einstein equation, not merely Newtonian confirmation.

name: Topological Channel --- V.D59
summary: The topological channel at x in tau^3 is the set of paths in defect space that increase d_top(x). It is closed when all such paths require energy input, and open when at least one path is energetically favorable.

name: Core Collapse Trigger --- V.T41
summary: The topological channel opens (core collapse begins) when the iron core mass satisfies M_c >= M_Ch^eff = M_Ch (1 - f_ec(Y_e)), where f_ec(Y_e) accounts for electron capture reducing the effective Chandrasekhar threshold below 1.44 M_sun.

name: The trigger is structural, not thermal
summary: In the tau-framework, core collapse is triggered structurally by the opening of the topological channel (allowing the system to access a lower-energy state with d_top > 0), not by thermal pressure loss. Electron capture and photodisintegration are readouts of this opening.

name: Channel Reversal --- V.D60
summary: A channel reversal occurs when the topological channel that was favorable in one direction (increasing d_top) becomes unfavorable, and the reverse direction becomes favorable. At the bounce, C-sector nuclear pressure exceeds D-sector compression, redirecting collapse energy outward.

name: Neutrino Heating Condition --- V.P37
summary: The stalled supernova shock is revived when the neutrino heating rate exceeds the cooling rate in the gain region. The heating depends on A-sector (Weak) neutrinos interacting with C-sector (Strong) nucleons via kappa(A,C) = iota_tau^4/(1-iota_tau).

name: Explosion energy and the mass cut
summary: The supernova explosion energy ~10^51 erg (~1% of total neutrino energy). The mass cut separating infalling from ejected material is a phase boundary between regions where d_top increases (infall) and where d_top decreases (ejection).

name: Plateau Duration as Readout --- V.P38
summary: The Type IIP supernova plateau duration t_plateau ~ M_env^{1/2} E_exp^{-1/6} R_0^{1/6} kappa^{1/6} is a readout of the hydrogen envelope mass, explosion energy, progenitor radius, and opacity, with constants determined by iota_tau and m_n.

name: r-Process as Rapid Charge Loading --- V.D61
summary: The r-process is the rapid sequential addition of C-sector (Strong) charge units (neutrons) to a seed nucleus: (Z,A) + n -> (Z, A+1) + gamma, repeated ~10-100 times faster than beta-decay (A-sector charge conversion), populating heavy element configurations.

name: Gold and the r-process
summary: Gold (Z=79) is an r-process element produced in neutron-rich ejecta of supernovae and neutron star mergers. In Category tau, each gold atom is a persistent defect bundle on T^2 whose (gamma, eta, omega)-charges were set by the nuclear physics of Book IV.

name: Proto-Neutron Star Evolution --- V.P39
summary: Proto-neutron star evolution is governed by three A-sector processes: neutrino diffusion (t_diff ~ 3-5 s), deleptonization (Y_e decreases as trapped neutrinos escape), and cooling, resulting in a cold degenerate neutron star.

name: Birth Kick and Spin --- V.P40
summary: Neutron stars receive natal kicks of ~200-500 km/s from asymmetries in neutrino emission or the explosion mechanism, a consequence of broken spherical symmetry in the channel reversal driven by SASI or convective overturn.

name: The carbon connection
summary: The carbon atoms in the reader's body were synthesized in the helium-burning shell of a star that exploded before the Sun formed. In Category tau, each carbon atom is a defect bundle on T^2 whose charges were determined by Book IV nuclear physics.

name: Equatorial focusing
summary: For a coherent instance with toroidal topology T^2 and shape ratio r/R = iota_tau, the effective gravitational potential has a minimum in the equatorial plane, producing a restoring force toward theta = pi/2 that focuses infalling matter.

name: Accretion Funnel (tau-geodesic)
summary: The accretion funnel of a coherent instance with toroidal boundary L is the region of tau^3 where infalling geodesics converge toward the equatorial plane of T^2, with angular momentum transport through the lemniscate crossing controlled by kappa(omega) = iota_tau^3/(1+iota_tau).

name: The alpha-viscosity re-read
summary: The Shakura-Sunyaev alpha-viscosity parameter, traditionally free at ~0.01-0.1, is determined in the tau-framework as the crossing coupling alpha_SS ~ kappa(omega) = iota_tau^3/(1+iota_tau) ~ 0.0297, squarely within the observationally inferred range.

name: Jet Axis (Topological Channel)
summary: The jet axis of a coherent instance is the one-dimensional subspace of tau^3 aligned with the base generator alpha, located within the torus hole (r < r_hole = R(1-iota_tau)), with two components (north/south poles) corresponding to bipolar jet lobes.

name: Jet-Torus Alignment
summary: The jet axis is exactly perpendicular to the equatorial plane of the accretion funnel, coinciding with the torus T^2 symmetry axis. The alignment is topological with no free parameter controlling the jet-disk angle.

name: Jet Collimation Theorem
summary: The jet opening half-angle satisfies sin(theta_jet) <= r/R = iota_tau ~ 0.3415, giving theta_jet <= 20 degrees. The bound arises from the torus geometry: trajectories within the jet channel must fit inside the torus hole.

name: Observational comparison
summary: Observed AGN jet opening half-angles range from ~1 degree (collimated FR II jets) to ~15-20 degrees (FR I jets), consistent with the tau-predicted upper bound of 20 degrees; greater collimation results from dynamical compression at high accretion rates.

name: Accretion Luminosity Bound
summary: Accretion luminosity L_acc = eta_acc M_dot c^2 <= L_Edd, where the non-spinning accretion efficiency eta_acc ~ iota_tau^2/3 ~ 4% arises from the ratio of Schwarzschild radius to ISCO radius, and the Eddington luminosity bounds the output.

name: Eddington Limit Re-Read
summary: The Eddington limit is structural in Category tau: it is the luminosity at which boundary-character radiation from the accretion funnel saturates the capacity of the D-sector to confine the inflow, not merely a radiation pressure coincidence.

name: Synchrotron Readout
summary: The synchrotron readout of an accretion funnel is the EM spectral power P_sync(nu) = (sqrt(3) e^3 B / (m_e c^2)) F(nu/nu_c) emitted by charged defect bundles spiraling in the gamma-sector field, with the sqrt(3) factor being the lemniscate spectral distance |1-omega|.

name: The ubiquitous sqrt3
summary: The sqrt(3) factor in the synchrotron power formula has the same origin as in the mass ratio correction: the spectral distance |1-omega| = sqrt(3) between adjacent lemniscate sectors (omega = e^{2 pi i/3}), unifying the synchrotron and electron mass formulas geometrically.

name: Neutrino Emission Channel
summary: In the inner accretion funnel where temperature exceeds T_W ~ m_W c^2/k_B ~ 10^15 K, neutrino pair production becomes efficient with luminosity L_nu ~ kappa(A,B) L_acc = iota_tau^3 eta_acc M_dot c^2 ~ 4% of accretion luminosity.

name: IceCube neutrinos from AGN
summary: The IceCube detection of high-energy neutrinos from blazar TXS 0506+056 is consistent with the tau-prediction that ~4% of accretion luminosity emerges as neutrinos, within current measurement uncertainties.

name: AGN Lifecycle Phase
summary: The AGN lifecycle phase is the pair (M_dot/M_dot_Edd, theta_obs): the normalized accretion rate encodes the evolutionary state (quasar -> Seyfert -> LINER -> quiescent) and the observation angle determines the apparent type (Type 1 vs Type 2).

name: AGN Unification Theorem
summary: All AGN are coherent instances of tau^3 with nonzero accretion rate. The observational taxonomy (Seyfert 1/2, quasar, blazar, FR I/II, LINER) is determined entirely by two parameters: normalized accretion rate and observation angle. No additional physical parameter is needed.

name: Quasar Redshift Distribution
summary: The quasar redshift distribution peaks at z ~ 2-3, corresponding in the tau-framework to the epoch of maximal boundary holonomy density and peak coherent-instance formation. The decline at z < 2 reflects consumption of the available holonomy budget.

name: Why the exponent is sim 4
summary: The exponent ~4 in the M-sigma relation is close to the effective gravitational dimension of tau^3 (topological dimension 3 plus temporal base tau^1 direction = 4). Dimensional analysis with virial theorem and jet-regulated accretion naturally produces exponent 4.

name: Binary Coherent-Instance System
summary: A binary coherent-instance system is a pair (I_1, I_2) of coherent instances on tau^3 sharing the same base tau^1 progression (same temporal depth), with mutual boundary holonomy H_12 governing their gravitational interaction.

name: GW Boundary-Character Wave
summary: A gravitational-wave boundary-character wave is a time-dependent perturbation delta H^D(t) of the D-sector boundary holonomy algebra, propagating at speed c. The two polarizations (plus and cross) correspond to the two traceless symmetric deformation modes of the fiber T^2.

name: Why two polarizations, not six
summary: The fiber T^2 is two-dimensional, so its traceless symmetric deformations form a two-dimensional space, directly yielding two GW polarization modes. No gauge fixing is needed, unlike GR where 10 metric components must be reduced to 2.

name: Chirp Mass from Boundary Holonomy
summary: The GW frequency evolution f_dot = (96/5) pi^{8/3} (G M_c/c^3)^{5/3} f^{11/3} during binary inspiral is governed by the chirp mass M_c = (M_1 M_2)^{3/5}/(M_1+M_2)^{1/5}, derived from boundary holonomy energy loss.

name: GW Energy Flux
summary: The gravitational-wave energy flux at distance d is F_GW = (c^3/(16 pi G)) <h_dot_+^2 + h_dot_x^2> = (c^3/(16 pi iota_tau^2)) (hbar/c^2) <...>, expressed in tau-native form using G = (c^3/hbar) iota_tau^2.

name: LIGO GW150914 Re-Read
summary: GW150914 is re-read as the coalescence of two coherent instances (36 M_sun + 29 M_sun -> 62 M_sun): the 3 M_sun energy deficit was radiated as D-sector boundary-character oscillations, with peak luminosity ~3.6e49 W exceeding all stellar luminosity in the observable universe.

name: Ringdown Readout
summary: The ringdown readout of a merged coherent instance is the quasi-normal mode decomposition h(t) = sum A_n exp(-t/tau_n) cos(2 pi f_n t + phi_n), where each mode has a frequency f_n and damping time tau_n determined by the final mass M_f.

name: Ringdown Uniqueness Theorem
summary: The quasi-normal mode spectrum {(f_n, tau_n)} of a coherent instance is determined uniquely by the mass M_f of the merged instance. No other parameter (holonomy configuration, perturbation shape) affects frequencies or damping times -- the tau-analogue of the no-hair theorem.

name: No-hair test via ringdown
summary: The Ringdown Uniqueness Theorem provides a direct test: if multiple QNM modes are measured from a single ringdown, their frequency ratio must match tau-Schwarzschild predictions. Any deviation would indicate additional parameters or departure from T^2 topology.

name: Standard Siren (tau)
summary: A standard siren is a binary merger whose GW signal provides a direct luminosity distance measurement d_L from the chirp mass and observed strain, requiring no empirical calibration step: the intrinsic amplitude is determined by M_c alone.

name: Multi-Messenger GW170817
summary: GW170817 (the first NS-NS standard siren with EM counterpart) gave d_L = 40 Mpc, host galaxy redshift z = 0.0098, yielding H_0 = 70 km/s/Mpc consistent with both CMB (67.4) and local (73.0) values within uncertainties.

name: Merger Graviton Count
summary: The graviton count from a binary BH merger N_graviton ~ E_GW/(hbar omega_peak) ~ eta_GW M c^2 / (hbar 2 pi f_peak), where f_peak ~ c^3/(GM), providing an estimate of the quantum granularity of the classical GW signal.

name: Toroidal Shadow (T^2 Readout)
summary: The toroidal shadow of a coherent instance with T^2 topology, major radius R, minor radius r = iota_tau R, viewed at inclination iota, is the closed curve on the observer's sky defined by critical impact parameters of null geodesics around the photon torus.

name: Shadow Shape Theorem (T^2 vs S^2)
summary: The shadow of a T^2 coherent instance differs from a Schwarzschild (S^2) shadow in three measurable ways: inclination-dependent ellipticity, an inner shadow (visible when the torus hole is face-on), and modified photon-ring spacing from the toroidal Lyapunov exponent.

name: Inner Shadow Ellipticity
summary: When the torus hole is visible (iota < iota_crit), the inner shadow has angular radius theta_inner ~ R(1-iota_tau) sqrt(1-iota_tau^2) cos(iota)/d_L and is circular regardless of viewing angle, providing a qualitative T^2 vs S^2 discriminant.

name: Photon Ring Winding Number
summary: The photon ring winding number n is the number of half-orbits a photon completes around the photon torus before escaping to the observer. Subrings indexed by n are nested, with the n-th subring lying inside the (n-1)-th subring.

name: Photon Subring Spacing
summary: Photon subring angular separations decrease geometrically: theta_{n+1}/theta_n = exp(-gamma_Lyap), with gamma_Lyap ~ pi for S^2 and pi(1+delta_tau) for T^2 topology, giving ~6% relative difference in subring spacing between the two topologies.

name: Observational accessibility
summary: The n=0 and n=1 subrings are blended in current EHT images; n=2 is suppressed by ~exp(-2pi) ~ 0.002. A space-VLBI mission with ~10-30 Earth-diameter baselines could resolve n=1 from direct emission and measure the Lyapunov exponent to distinguish T^2 from S^2.

name: Polarization Handedness Signature
summary: The polarization handedness signature is the EVPA winding number w_EVPA = (1/2pi) oint dchi around the shadow boundary, encoding the magnetic field geometry. For toroidal topology with toroidal B-field, w_EVPA = 2; for radial B-field (S^2), w_EVPA = 1.

name: Polarization Winding Theorem
summary: For a coherent instance with T^2 topology and equatorial accretion flow, the EVPA winding number w_EVPA = 2 (toroidal field) vs w_EVPA = 1 (radial field, S^2), providing a polarimetric discriminant between toroidal and spherical BH topology.

name: M87* Shadow Re-Read
summary: The M87* shadow (diameter 42 +/- 3 mu-as) is consistent with the tau-Schwarzschild prediction theta_sh = 3 sqrt(3) GM/(c^2 d_L). Current polarimetric data cannot distinguish w_EVPA = 1 from w_EVPA = 2 within measurement uncertainties.

name: Sgr A* Shadow Re-Read
summary: The Sgr A* shadow (~52 mu-as diameter) is more time-variable than M87* due to its ~1500x smaller mass and correspondingly shorter dynamical timescale (minutes vs weeks), consistent with tau-Schwarzschild for M = 4.3e6 M_sun at d = 8.3 kpc.

name: The strongest single test
summary: The inner shadow is the most discriminating single test: it is a qualitative difference (donut vs disk shadow) that would constitute strong evidence for toroidal topology if detected in a face-on compact object at sufficient EHT resolution.

name: Bullet Cluster Orthodox Argument
summary: The orthodox Bullet Cluster argument proceeds in three steps: X-ray imaging shows stripped gas, weak lensing shows mass centered on galaxies not gas, and the offset is attributed to collisionless dark matter passing through while gas is decelerated.

name: Boundary-Mass Offset (tau)
summary: The boundary-mass offset Delta x = |x_lensing - x_gas| is the spatial separation between the baryonic mass centroid (X-ray gas) and the total gravitational mass centroid (weak lensing), arising because boundary holonomy mass M_partial concentrates at coherent instances.

name: Bullet Cluster Without Dark Matter
summary: The Bullet Cluster lensing-gas offset is explained by boundary holonomy mass: total gravitational mass = particle mass M_p + boundary holonomy mass M_partial. Galaxies carry M_partial collisionlessly through the merger while gas (M_p) is stripped by ram pressure.

name: Lensing-Gas Offset Bound
summary: In any cluster merger with relative velocity v and gas fraction f_g, the lensing-gas offset satisfies Delta x <= v t_cross (1 - f_g/(1 + M_partial/M_p)), bounded by the crossing time and the ratio of boundary holonomy mass to particle mass.

name: Handle-Scale Event
summary: A handle-scale event is a dynamical process involving rearrangement of boundary holonomy at spatial scales comparable to the major radius R ~ R_S/iota_tau = 2GM/(iota_tau c^2) of the tau^3 fibration.

name: Wilson Skeleton (Cosmic Web)
summary: The Wilson skeleton W(L) at readout scale L is the network of holonomy loops on the base tau^1 with circumference <= L. Its image gives the cosmic web structure: nodes = clusters, edges = filaments, enclosed regions = voids.

name: Cosmic Web from Holonomy Loops
summary: The large-scale structure at scales L > 10 Mpc is isomorphic to the Wilson skeleton W(L) of the base tau^1: galaxy clusters correspond to nodes, filaments to edges (holonomy loop segments), and voids to enclosed regions of the skeleton.

name: BAO and Holonomy Periodicity
summary: The BAO scale is reinterpreted as the holonomy return length: the characteristic circumference of fundamental holonomy loops on tau^1 at the recombination epoch, with the sound horizon r_s being the arc length for one complete loop.

name: Topological Lensing Signature
summary: The topological lensing signature is the additional light deflection Delta theta_top = oint kappa_tau Omega_D ds accumulated along a light ray's path through the Wilson skeleton, beyond standard gravitational lensing, with kappa_tau = 1 - iota_tau.

name: Filament Scaling Relation
summary: The topological lensing contribution from a cosmic-web filament of length L_f and linear mass density mu_f scales as Delta theta_top ~ kappa_tau G mu_f L_f / (c^2 b_perp) ~ (1-iota_tau) iota_tau^2 mu_f L_f / (hbar b_perp), conjectural pending numerical evaluation.

name: Minimal Alphabet and Sector Count
summary: The Minimal Alphabet Theorem (I.T09) asserts that exactly five generators are needed: fewer are insufficient and more are redundant. A six-generator model always has the sixth expressible as a combination of the original five, fixing the sector count at exactly four plus crossing.

name: From "Not Needed" to "Not Possible"
summary: The 2nd Edition upgrades the dark matter argument from "not needed" (phenomenological, 1st Edition) to "not possible" (structural): the Sector Exhaustion Theorem proves that exactly five sectors exhaust the coupling budget with no room for a dark sector.

name: Sector Budget
summary: The sector budget B(n) = {kappa(X; n) | X in {A,B,C,D,omega}} partitions the total coupling into sector contributions at orbit depth n, subject to a normalization constraint analogous to a probability distribution (total sums to 1, all non-negative).

name: Coupling Budget Completeness
summary: At depth n=1, the five self-couplings are: kappa(D;1) = 1-iota_tau, kappa(A;1) = iota_tau, kappa(B;2) = iota_tau^2, kappa(C;3) = iota_tau^3/(1-iota_tau), kappa(omega) = iota_tau^3/(1+iota_tau), exhausting the total coupling budget.

name: Sector Exhaustion Decomposition
summary: The sector exhaustion decomposition H_partial[omega] = H_A + H_B + H_C + H_D + H_omega is the direct-sum decomposition of the boundary holonomy algebra into its four primitive sector components plus the crossing component, with mutually orthogonal sectors.

name: Sector Exhaustion Theorem
summary: The sector exhaustion decomposition is exhaustive: every element of H_partial[omega] is a sum of components from H_A, H_B, H_C, H_D, and H_omega with no remainder, and the decomposition is unique (the sectors are linearly independent).

name: Dark Sector Hypothesis
summary: The dark sector hypothesis posits a nonzero tau-algebra component H_dark that is EM-invisible (no B-sector coupling), gravitationally active (D-sector coupling), and not contained in any of the five sectors {H_A, H_B, H_C, H_D, H_omega}.

name: No Fifth Sector
