summary: The dark sector hypothesis is false: no component satisfying all three conditions (EM-invisible, gravitationally active, outside the five sectors) exists in the boundary holonomy algebra of any tau^3 fibration, as a direct consequence of sector exhaustion.

name: No Dark Matter Particle
summary: No particle in Category tau has dark matter properties (massive, stable, non-baryonic, not a known lepton): any massive particle must reside in one of the five sectors, and systematic elimination (D = base-only, B/C = baryonic, A = known, omega = Higgs) excludes all candidates.

name: No Dark Energy Field
summary: No field in Category tau has dark energy properties (uniform, gravitationally repulsive, not in any known sector): any such field must reside in one of the five sectors, and none produces a uniform repulsive effect. Accelerated expansion is a readout artifact.

name: No Fifth Force
summary: No fundamental interaction beyond the four forces (Gravity D, Weak A, EM B, Strong C) and Higgs mechanism (omega) exists in Category tau. The five sectors are exhaustive; a fifth force would require a sixth generator, which the Minimal Alphabet Theorem forbids.

name: What 68% and 27% Actually Are
summary: In the tau-framework, the Lambda-CDM 5% baryonic matter is particle mass M_p, the 27% dark matter is boundary holonomy mass M_partial (not a particle), and the 68% dark energy is a readout artifact from projecting depth-dependent progression onto the Friedmann model.

name: Readout Artifact
summary: A readout artifact is a quantity that appears nonzero in the orthodox readout (tau-native structure projected onto a classical manifold) but corresponds to no independent substance or field in Category tau. Dark energy (Omega_Lambda ~ 0.68) is the principal readout artifact.

name: LCDM Budget Translation
summary: The Lambda-CDM budget translates to tau as: Omega_b ~ 0.05 <-> M_p/M_total ~ 0.15, Omega_DM ~ 0.27 <-> M_partial/M_total (boundary holonomy mass), Omega_Lambda ~ 0.68 <-> readout artifact (no physical field).

name: Orbit-Depth-Dependent Readout
summary: An orbit-depth-dependent readout is a physical quantity whose measured value depends on the orbit depth n at which measurement is performed, defined as the restriction of an observable O on H_partial[omega] to the level-n truncation of the refinement tower.

name: Depth-Projected Hubble Parameter
summary: The depth-projected Hubble parameter H(n) = H_infty (1 + delta H / iota_tau^n) approaches H_infty at large n (early universe) and takes larger values at small n (late universe), with the depth correction delta H driven by the defect structure of the progression operator.

name: Both Measurements Correct
summary: Both H_0 measurements are correct: the early-universe measurement (Planck, z ~ 1100) probes H(n_CMB) ~ H_infty ~ 67.4 km/s/Mpc, while the local measurement probes H(n_local) > H_infty due to the depth correction delta H / iota_tau^{n_local}.

name: H0 Tension Resolution
summary: The H_0 tension is resolved by the orbit-depth dependence of the Hubble parameter: H(n) is monotonically decreasing with n, so H(n_local) > H(n_CMB). The ~8% discrepancy between 67.4 and 73.0 km/s/Mpc is the expected depth correction, with no free parameters.

name: Depth-Hubble Monotonicity
summary: The depth correction delta H ~ H_infty iota_tau^{n_0}, where n_0 is the transition depth from the deep-tower to the local-readout regime. For the observed 8% discrepancy, the monotonicity of H(n) (guaranteed by positivity of delta H) is a conjectural quantitative prediction.

name: Category Error (LCDM)
summary: Lambda-CDM commits three category errors: identifying boundary holonomy mass as a new matter particle (dark matter as substance), the progression rate as a cosmological constant (dark energy as field), and the readout projection as a fundamental spacetime manifold.

name: Correct Parameters Wrong Ontology
summary: The Lambda-CDM parameters (Omega_m, Omega_Lambda, H_0, n_s, sigma_8, tau_reion) are correct as best-fit parameters of the Friedmann model to observational data. The tau-framework does not contradict the data; it contradicts the ontological story Lambda-CDM tells about the data.

name: Readout Projection Table
summary: The readout projection table translates each Lambda-CDM parameter into its tau-native counterpart, preserving numerical values while changing ontological interpretation: same numbers, different story.

name: Sigma8 as Defect Amplitude
summary: The fluctuation amplitude sigma_8 (density variance smoothed on 8 h^{-1} Mpc) is reinterpreted as the variance of the boundary holonomy concentration rho_partial(x) over the Wilson skeleton W(L) at scale L = 8 h^{-1} Mpc.

name: Dark Sector Closure
summary: The dark sector of Lambda-CDM is dissolved: no dark matter particle exists (all excess gravitational mass is M_partial), no dark energy field exists (accelerated expansion is a readout artifact), and no fifth force exists (five sectors are exhaustive).

name: What Would Reopen the Case
summary: The Dark Sector Closure Theorem is falsifiable: direct detection of a non-baryonic massive stable particle, observation of a fifth fundamental force, or demonstration that boundary holonomy mass cannot reproduce observed lensing-rotation concordance would reopen the case.

name: From Part V to Part VI
summary: Part V established the global structure: no dark matter, no dark energy, no fifth force, no H_0 tension, five exhaustive sectors. Part VI (Eternal Dynamics) extends to extreme regimes: Big Bang as regime boundary, inflation as regime, BH topology, and the falsification pack.

name: No manifold Rightarrow no singularity
summary: GR singularities require smooth manifolds with no minimum scale; tau^3 is a profinite space with first element alpha_1, so the limit a -> 0 is structurally inaccessible and no singularity analogue exists. The Penrose-Hawking premises simply do not apply.

name: Temporal Opening
summary: The temporal opening is the structural transition from the pre-temporal kernel specification to the first refinement level alpha_1 = alpha. It is unique (exactly one first level), irreversible (no pre-alpha_1 state), and maximal in boundary-character energy density.

name: Planck Epoch Reinterpretation
summary: Reinterprets the Planck epoch (t < t_P) as the first few alpha-ticks alpha_1, alpha_2, ... governed by the same tau-Einstein equation as all later ticks. The physics is not unknown; the boundary characters simply take extreme near-saturation values at early depths.

name: Pre-Hadronic Regime
summary: The pre-hadronic regime R_pre is the interval of alpha-ticks from the temporal opening alpha_1 to the neutron threshold L_N, i.e., {alpha_n : 1 <= n < n_N}. Before n_N, no stable composite nucleons exist as persistent defect bundles on T^2.

name: Regime Boundary Character
summary: A regime boundary character chi_n at refinement depth n is the restriction chi_n = ev_n composed with chi in H_partial[omega]_n, where ev_n evaluates the full boundary character at depth n. It captures the spectral content available at a given refinement level.

name: Same-Equation Proposition
summary: The tau-Einstein equation R^H = kappa_tau * T applies identically in the pre-hadronic, hadronic, and present-epoch regimes. It contains no regime-dependent coupling constants, field content, or spacetime dimension. Only the boundary characters differ between regimes.

name: No-Singularity Theorem
summary: No cosmological singularity exists in Category tau: the profinite boundary holonomy algebra H_partial[omega] has bounded norm at every depth, the source term T[chi_n] is bounded, and the alpha-orbit has a first element alpha_1 with no sub-Planckian descent.

name: The Penrose--Hawking theorems are not wrong
summary: The Penrose-Hawking singularity theorems are mathematically correct within their domain (smooth Lorentzian manifolds satisfying energy conditions), but their premise does not hold in tau: the profinite fibered product tau^3 replaces the smooth manifold assumption entirely.

name: Big Bang = Opening Regime
summary: The Big Bang is the opening regime of the tau-Einstein equation: same equation at all depths, extreme boundary characters at early ticks that produce high-density readouts, and a three-phase structure (pre-hadronic, hadronic transition, late-time gravitational dominance).

name: No ``hot'' or ``cold''
summary: Temperature is a chart-level readout concept requiring thermal equilibrium. At very early alpha-ticks, no meaningful thermal equilibrium exists. The language of "hot Big Bang" is a chart-level approximation accurate only after the first regime transitions.

name: Falsifiability
summary: The tau-framework makes falsifiable early-universe predictions that differ from orthodox cosmology: no primordial singularity gravitational-wave background, specific CMB deviations. Differences are small (agreeing with LambdaCDM to leading order) but measurable by CMB-S4 and LiteBIRD.

name: Regime Invariance
summary: A dynamical equation on tau^1 is regime-invariant if its structural form -- the algebraic identity relating curvature, coupling, and source -- is independent of the refinement depth n. The tau-Einstein equation's structure does not change across cosmological epochs.

name: Regime Invariance Theorem
summary: The tau-Einstein equation R^H[chi_{n+1}] = kappa_tau * T[chi_n] is regime-invariant: it holds for all refinement depths n >= 1 with the same coupling kappa_tau and the same algebraic structure, because the fibered product decomposition tau^3 = tau^1 x_f T^2 is depth-independent.

name: Contrast with running couplings
summary: In the Standard Model, couplings run with energy scale (e.g., asymptotic freedom). In tau, kappa_tau is fixed; what changes is the boundary character entering the equation. Apparent running in chart-level readouts is a projection artifact from coarse-graining the profinite structure.

name: Inflaton No-Go Corollary
summary: No inflaton field exists in Category tau. No new scalar sector can be added beyond the five sectors {D, A, B, C, omega} without violating Sector Exhaustion, and no new fundamental field exists beyond the five generators alpha, pi, gamma, eta, omega.

name: Inflationary Regime
summary: The inflationary regime is the sub-interval of the pre-hadronic regime during which the chart-level readout yields approximately exponential expansion: a(alpha_{n+1})/a(alpha_n) > e^delta. It begins at the temporal opening and ends when boundary characters decrease below the threshold.

name: e-Fold Readout
summary: The e-fold readout N_e is the total number of e-folds accumulated during the inflationary regime: N_e = sum over n in R_inf of ln(a(alpha_{n+1})/a(alpha_n)). The primorial structure naturally produces N_e >> 60, far exceeding the orthodox minimum requirement.

name: Slow Roll Unnecessary
summary: In orthodox inflation, the slow-roll condition epsilon << 1 constrains the inflaton potential to be flat. In tau, no slow-roll is needed: regime invariance guarantees the same equation governs inflation, and exponential expansion follows from near-saturation boundary characters, not a flat potential.

name: Flatness from Compactness
summary: Spatial curvature Omega_k = 0 exactly in tau, as a structural result: the fiber T^2 is a compact torus with zero Gaussian curvature, and the chart-level readout inherits this flatness. This requires no fine-tuning or inflationary epoch -- it is a geometric consequence of toroidal fiber structure.

name: Horizon Resolution
summary: The horizon problem does not arise in tau. The base circle tau^1 is compact (all points at finite distance from alpha_1), the fiber T^2 is compact (all spatial points at finite mutual distance), so tau^3 is compact and every point is in causal contact with every other point.

name: Compactness vs. inflation
summary: Orthodox inflation solves the horizon problem dynamically (rapid expansion stretches a small region) requiring a specific inflaton mechanism. Tau-compactness solves it structurally: the universe is compact at all times, so causally disconnected regions never exist. The mechanism is geometric, not dynamical.

name: A falsifiable prediction
summary: The tensor-to-scalar ratio prediction r ~ iota_tau^4 ~ 0.014 is specific and falsifiable. It lies below the current BICEP/Keck bound r < 0.036 and within reach of LiteBIRD and CMB-S4. Tension arises if r > 0.05 or r < 0.001 is measured.

name: Threshold (Regime Boundary)
summary: A threshold at refinement depth n_* is a critical depth where a qualitative change occurs in the regime boundary character chi_n: a sector coupling or cross-coupling function f_X(chi_n) crosses a critical value f_X^crit, producing a phase transition in the physical readout.

name: Canonical Thresholds
summary: The canonical threshold ladder is the ordered sequence of six regime transitions: L_EW (electroweak) -> L_B (baryogenesis) -> L_N (neutron) -> L_nuc (nucleosynthesis) -> L_H (hydrogen recombination) -> L_gamma (photon decoupling, z ~ 1100).

name: Ladder Monotonicity
summary: The canonical thresholds occur in the order n_EW < n_B < n_N < n_nuc < n_H < n_gamma, equivalently in order of decreasing chart-level temperature. Each threshold is a crossing condition on a monotonically decreasing function of refinement depth.

name: Neutron Threshold L_N
summary: The neutron threshold L_N is the refinement depth n_N at which the Strong-sector (eta) character drops below the confinement critical value kappa(C; 3) = iota_tau^3/(1 - iota_tau), and stable composite nucleons appear as persistent defect bundles on T^2.

name: The mass hierarchy at L_N
summary: At the neutron threshold L_N, the mass hierarchy m_n > m_p > m_e is established with m_p = m_n - delta_A and m_e = m_n/R, where delta_A/m_n ~ (sqrt(3)/2)*iota_tau^6 and R = iota_tau^{-7} - (sqrt(3) + pi^3*alpha^2)*iota_tau^{-2}. All three masses follow from m_n and iota_tau alone.

name: Sphaleron Open Question
summary: The tau-analogue of the sphaleron process would be a non-perturbative crossing between the Weak sector (A) and Strong sector (C) at the omega-crossing point. Whether this process generates sufficient baryon asymmetry remains an open question at the conjectural scope level.

name: Sakharov Conditions
summary: Two of the three Sakharov conditions for baryogenesis are structural in tau: CP violation from the bipolar lemniscate, and departure from equilibrium from the directed alpha-orbit. Only baryon number violation remains open, reducing the mystery from three unknown mechanisms to one.

name: Nucleosynthetic Window
summary: The nucleosynthetic window W_nuc is the interval of refinement depths [n_nuc^open, n_nuc^close] during which light nuclei can be synthesized: deuterium survives above the open threshold, and the window closes when the expansion rate exceeds the nuclear reaction rate.

name: Nucleosynthesis from tau
summary: The nucleosynthetic window produces observed light-element abundances: He-4 mass fraction Y_p ~ 0.245 from the neutron-to-proton ratio at L_N controlled by delta_A and kappa(A;1), and deuterium abundance from EM and Strong sector couplings. Nuclear physics is identical; only the cosmological framework differs.

name: The lithium problem
summary: The cosmological lithium problem (discrepancy between predicted and observed primordial Li-7 abundance) is inherited by tau unchanged from standard BBN, because the nuclear physics is identical. Resolution requires modified nuclear cross sections or late-time depletion, not cosmological modification.

name: CMB Origin
summary: The CMB is the photon readout of the last-scattering surface at the photon decoupling threshold L_gamma. In tau, the last-scattering surface is the fiber slice T^2 at depth n_gamma, and T_CMB ~ 2.725 K is the readout of the boundary character at that depth.

name: Threshold Ladder
summary: The threshold ladder is the complete ordered sequence of canonical thresholds together with the regime boundary characters at each threshold and the physical readout that changes at each crossing, providing a unified description of all cosmological phase transitions.

name: Gravitational Tension
summary: Gravitational tension at a region U in tau^3 is G(U) = kappa(D;1) * ||T[chi]|_U||, where kappa(D;1) = 1 - iota_tau is the gravitational self-coupling and ||.|| is the boundary norm on H_partial[omega]. It measures the gravitational field strength generated by matter in U.

name: Spherical Capacity
summary: The spherical capacity C_sph(n) of the fiber T^2 at base point alpha_n is the supremum of gravitational tension G(U) over all contractible subsets U of T^2. It is the maximum gravitational tension the fiber can sustain while remaining topologically contractible.

name: Linking Class
summary: A linking class in tau^3 is a non-contractible cycle ell in H_1(T^2; Z) = Z + Z that links the two generators of pi_1(T^2). It is non-trivial if both components ell = (a, b) satisfy ab != 0, wrapping around both cycles of the torus fiber.

name: Black Hole (Topological Event)
summary: A black hole in Category tau is the emergence of a non-trivial linking class ell in H_1(T^2; Z) at a base point alpha_{n_*} where the gravitational tension exceeds the spherical capacity: G(U_{n_*}) > C_sph(n_*). The BH is a topological event, not a metric singularity.

name: BH Threshold Theorem
summary: A black hole forms if and only if the gravitational tension at some region U exceeds the spherical capacity: G(U) > C_sph. At the chart level, this reduces to the Schwarzschild criterion. The theorem gives a necessary and sufficient topological condition for BH formation.

name: BH Toroidal Topology
summary: The horizon of a tau-black hole is topologically T^2 (torus), not S^2 (sphere). The linking class ell in H_1(T^2; Z) wraps both cycles of the fiber, the horizon IS the fiber at the excision region, and the two fundamental cycles correspond to the gamma and eta generators.

name: Event horizon as linking boundary
summary: The event horizon in tau is the linking boundary: the boundary of the T^2 region enclosed by the non-trivial linking class. Inside the linking boundary, the fiber topology is non-contractible; outside, it is contractible. This is the tau-native definition replacing the GR causal boundary.

name: No Interior Singularity
summary: A tau-black hole has no interior singularity. The interior is a compact subset of T^2 with all boundary characters bounded (by the No-Singularity Theorem). The curvature functional R^H[chi] remains finite everywhere inside the linking class enclosure.

name: Information Preservation
summary: No information is lost in a tau-black hole. The boundary holonomy algebra H_partial[omega] as an inverse system preserves all data through the coherence condition. The linking class encodes the full state, and interior information is recoverable from exterior boundary characters.

name: Canonical BH Neighborhood
summary: The canonical BH neighborhood N_BH is the open subset of tau^3 consisting of all points (alpha_n, x) with n >= n_* (birth depth) and gravitational tension G(alpha_n, x) > C_sph. It is the spacetime region occupied by the black hole from birth onward.

name: BH Bipolarity
summary: A black hole with linking class ell = (a,b) is bipolar if its boundary character chi_BH restricted to the linking boundary has nonzero components on both lobes: chi_BH^+ != 0 and chi_BH^- != 0. Bipolarity reflects the chi_+/chi_- spectral decomposition of the lemniscate.

name: Necessary Bipolarity
summary: Every black hole in Category tau is bipolar; unipolar black holes (with chi_BH^+ = 0 or chi_BH^- = 0) do not exist. This follows from the non-trivial linking class requiring both components ab != 0, which forces coupling to both lobes of the lemniscate boundary.

name: Polarity Imbalance
summary: The polarity imbalance I_BH = (||chi_BH^+|| - ||chi_BH^-||)/(||chi_BH^+|| + ||chi_BH^-||) in (-1,1) measures asymmetry between the two lemniscate lobes. I = 0 is perfect bipolarity; the necessary bipolarity theorem prevents I from reaching the unipolar limits +/-1.

name: Polarity Convergence
summary: As a black hole evolves and absorbs matter, its polarity imbalance converges to the fixed point lim I_BH(alpha_n) = 1 - 2*iota_tau ~ 0.317. The master constant iota_tau determines the asymptotic balance between the two lemniscate lobes for all mature black holes.

name: Blueprint
summary: The blueprint of a black hole is the pair b_BH = (chi_BH^+, chi_BH^-) in H_partial^+ x H_partial^- of boundary characters restricted to the linking boundary. It encodes the full coherence tower at the horizon, generalizing the classical no-hair characterization (M, J, Q).

name: Blueprint fusion mathrmFuse
summary: Blueprint fusion Fuse_omega combines two blueprints: Fuse_omega(b_1, b_2) = (chi_1^+ * chi_2^+, chi_1^- * chi_2^-), where * is the product in the boundary holonomy algebra mediated by the omega-germ. It models black hole merger at the algebraic level.

name: Blueprint Monoid
summary: The blueprint monoid M_BH = ({blueprints}, Fuse_omega, b_vac) has carrier set of all blueprints, fusion as the binary operation, and vacuum blueprint b_vac = (1^+, 1^-) as identity. It provides the algebraic structure governing black hole combinations.

name: Blueprint Monoid Closure
summary: The blueprint monoid M_BH is a well-defined monoid: Fuse_omega is closed (fusion of blueprints yields a blueprint), associative, has identity b_vac, but is NOT invertible -- the boundary holonomy algebra contains nilpotent elements, so Fuse_omega^{-1} does not exist for all blueprints.

name: Irreversibility of mergers
summary: The non-invertibility of the blueprint monoid is the algebraic expression of irreversibility of BH mergers. Two black holes can merge but cannot split back. This replaces Hawking's area increase theorem with a more fundamental algebraic statement: the monoid structure enforces merger irreversibility.

name: BH Entropy Formula Interpretation
summary: The BH entropy formula becomes S_BH = k_B * A/(4*iota_tau^2), replacing the Planck length l_P^2 with iota_tau^2. This is not merely notational: it makes BH entropy a statement about the master constant, connecting it to the boundary-character counting on the profinite tower.

name: Export to Book VI
summary: The blueprint monoid M_BH is the primary algebraic structure exported from Book V to Book VI (Categorical Life). In Book VI, it models metabolic processing (matter absorption as fusion), replication (blueprint copying), and structural memory (coherence tower persistence).

name: Mature Black Hole
summary: A black hole is mature at orbit depth n if: (1) geometric stabilization -- the linking class ell is rho-invariant, and (2) topological relaxation -- the boundary character has converged to its fixed-point configuration. Maturity means the BH geometry and topology have settled.

name: Defect-Mass Coupling
summary: For a mature black hole, any mass decrease M' < M would produce a non-zero defect: S_def^BH(M') > S_def^BH(M) = 0. Reducing mass from the equilibrium state creates a transient defect, coupling mass to the defect functional in a one-directional constraint.

name: No-Shrink Theorem
summary: No-Shrink Theorem: for any mature black hole with M >= M_min (Chandrasekhar limit), dM/dn >= 0. No tau-admissible evolution step can decrease the mass of a mature black hole. The proof follows from defect-mass coupling: mass loss would create inadmissible positive defect entropy.

name: Hawking Readout
summary: Hawking radiation is a chart-level readout of the boundary character chi_BH at the linking boundary, not a transport of mass-energy from interior to infinity. The quantum-fields-on-curved-spacetime framework is an E_1 chart describing empirical appearance, not ontic mass loss.

name: No BH Evaporation
summary: No black hole evaporates. The ontic mass of every mature black hole is monotonically non-decreasing: M(n+1) >= M(n) for all n >= n*. The evaporation time is an artifact of chart-level interpretation. Black holes are permanent ontic objects in Category tau.

name: Information Paradox Dissolved
summary: The information paradox dissolves in tau because assumption (1) -- that black holes evaporate -- is false. There is no information loss because there is no evaporation. This is not a mechanism for information escape (Page, Maldacena, Penington) but a removal of the paradox's premise.

name: Permanence Hallmark
summary: A permanence hallmark is a structural property P of a coherent instance in tau^3 such that: (1) there exists a finite acquisition threshold n_P at which P is first satisfied, and (2) for all n >= n_P, P continues to hold (irreversibility). BH linking class is the primary example.

name: Permanence Export to Book VI
summary: The permanence hallmark is the key structural export from Book V to Book VI. In Book VI, the "alive" predicate for a BH-based system requires at least one permanence hallmark: a system without permanence is transient, not alive. Three items exported: No-Shrink, linking class, blueprint.

name: BH Entropy Formula
summary: BH entropy S_BH = k_B * A/(4*iota_tau^2) is derived from boundary counting: the torus horizon T^2 with area A has boundary characters at each refinement level contributing log-microstates proportional to A/iota_tau^2, recovering the Bekenstein-Hawking formula with iota_tau replacing l_P.

name: Why overlap forces merger
summary: In tau, BH mergers are topological: two excisions cannot coexist when their boundaries overlap, because the coherence kernel (axiom K3, injectivity of rho) forbids two distinct topological defects at the same refinement-tower location. Overlap forces coalescence, unlike the continuous GR dynamics.

name: Merger Normal Form
summary: Merger Normal Form: when two single-excision BHs with masses M_1, M_2 and angular momenta J_1, J_2 satisfy the approach condition, the configuration converges to a unique single-excision fixed point with M_final = M_1 + M_2 - Delta_E/c^2. The final state is algebraically determined, not numerically simulated.

name: What the Normal Form does not give
summary: The Merger Normal Form determines the final state and total radiated energy Delta_E but does not provide the time-domain gravitational waveform h(t). The waveform requires chart-level inspiral/plunge/ringdown dynamics. The Normal Form is topological; the waveform is dynamical.

name: Ringdown mode
summary: The n-th ringdown mode of a merged excision is r_n(t) = A_n * e^{-sigma_n * t} * cos(omega_n * t + phi_n), with amplitude A_n, damping rate sigma_n from the spectral gap of rho, and frequency omega_n from the n-th harmonic of the stabilized linking class.

name: Ringdown damping is structural
summary: Every ringdown mode has strictly positive damping rate sigma_n > 0 for all n >= 1, so the ringdown terminates in finite proper time. The damping is structural: it follows from the spectral gap of the progression operator rho on higher harmonic subspaces and the Bounded Time Theorem.

name: BH mass scale at depth n
summary: The BH mass scale at refinement depth n is M_n = m_n * kappa(D;1) = m_n * (1 - iota_tau), where m_n is the total mass-energy content at depth n. At low depths the mass scales are large (supermassive BHs); at high depths they are small (stellar-mass BHs).

name: Mass gap between adjacent primorial levels
summary: The ratio of BH mass scales at adjacent primorial depths is M_n/M_{n+1} ~ q_{n+1}, where q_{n+1} is the (n+1)-th prime. The mass hierarchy M_1 >> M_2 >> M_3 >> ... has gaps growing with the primes, organized by the primorial structure of the refinement tower.

name: The mass gap and the IMBH desert
summary: The primorial mass hierarchy predicts a natural gap in the BH mass spectrum between supermassive (10^6-10^10 M_sun) and stellar-mass (3-100 M_sun) black holes, with intermediate-mass BHs (10^2-10^5 M_sun) scarce. This is consistent with the observational IMBH desert.

name: Scope note on mass spectrum predictions
summary: The qualitative BH mass spectrum features (hierarchy, gap, IMBH scarcity) follow at the tau-effective level from primorial structure. Specific numerical predictions (peak locations) require calibrating primorial depths to SI mass scales via the full calibration cascade, which remains conjectural.

name: Base Wilson loop
summary: The base Wilson loop W_n = Tr(Hol_partial(tau^1; n)) at refinement depth n is the trace of the boundary holonomy around the base circle tau^1 at depth n. It provides a non-perturbative probe of the gravitational sector geometry on the base.

name: Gravitational deconfinement
summary: The gravitational sector D is deconfined: base Wilson loops satisfy a perimeter law W_n ~ exp(-kappa_tau * L(tau^1; n)), not an area law. The base circle tau^1 is one-dimensional, so the gauge field is abelian at leading order and gravity does not confine.

name: Contrast with the strong sector
summary: Gravity (base tau^1) is deconfined with perimeter-law Wilson loops, while the strong force (fiber T^2) is confined with area-law Wilson loops. This contrast is a direct dimensional consequence: tau^1 is 1D (no area), T^2 is 2D (area law possible).

name: BH gravitational Aharonov--Bohm phase
summary: The gravitational Aharonov-Bohm phase of a BH with mass M is Phi_BH = GM/iota_tau = (c^3/hbar)*iota_tau*M. The phase is proportional to both the mass and the master constant iota_tau -- a topological phase from circling a gravitational flux tube, yielding a measurable prediction.

name: Radiated energy bound
summary: The total energy radiated during a quasi-circular non-spinning BH merger satisfies Delta_E/((M_1+M_2)c^2) <= 1 - 1/sqrt(2) ~ 0.293. For equal-mass mergers, numerical relativity gives ~0.05, well below this bound. The maximum is achieved only in the extreme-spin Kerr limit.

name: The 1/sqrt2
summary: The 1/sqrt(2) radiated energy bound is a classical GR result (Penrose extraction limit for Kerr BHs). In tau, the same bound follows from the entropy constraint: defect entropy S_def of the final excision must exceed the sum of initial defect entropies by the No-Shrink Theorem.

name: Topological motif
summary: A topological motif at depth n is an equivalence class [D]_n of defect tuple configurations on an open neighborhood U of tau^3, where configurations are equivalent under continuous deformation preserving defect-component thresholds. A motif must be stable (rho-invariant) and irreducible.

name: Finite Motif Theorem
summary: The number of distinct stable irreducible topological motifs in tau^3 is finite: N_motif(n) <= 2^4 * p_n^3 at each depth, and as n -> infinity the count stabilizes to a finite N_eternal. The four-component defect tuple on compact tau^3 admits only finitely many persistent configurations.

name: Contrast with fractal cosmologies
summary: The Finite Motif Theorem excludes fractal cosmologies: there is no infinite hierarchy of self-similar patterns at arbitrarily large or small scales. The motif count stabilizes at a finite value. The universe has a finite structural alphabet with no infinite descent or ascent.

name: Saturation radius
summary: The saturation radius R_sat is the smallest length scale such that every eternal motif appearing anywhere in tau^3 already appears within a ball of radius R_sat centered at any point. Beyond R_sat, no structurally new type of object (vacuum, star, galaxy, BH) can be found.

name: Saturation Radius Theorem
summary: The saturation radius R_sat is finite, bounded by R_sat <= C * t_infty * c where t_infty = sum p_k^{-1} is the total proper time. Beyond R_sat, the motif distribution is statistically periodic. The universe exhausts its structural vocabulary within a finite spatial extent.

name: Saturation radius vs. observable universe
summary: The saturation radius R_sat is a structural property of tau^3, while the observable universe is a chart-level concept. R_sat is comparable to the observable universe size, meaning what we can see is essentially everything there is in terms of structural variety.

name: Absorbing pattern
summary: An absorbing pattern P_infty: tau^3 -> M_eternal assigns to each point its limiting eternal motif. It is rho-invariant (the pattern is preserved by the progression operator) and locally constant on the chart-level geometry (no fluctuation below R_sat).

name: Absorbing Pattern Theorem
summary: The motif distribution on tau^3 converges to a unique absorbing pattern P_infty as refinement depth n -> infinity. On the compact torus T^2, rho acts as a contraction yielding convergence; on the base tau^1, the directed alpha-orbit forces monotone refinement without reversal.

name: No infinite hierarchy
summary: The Absorbing Pattern Theorem precisely negates "turtles all the way down": no infinite tower of ever-larger structures contains our observable universe, and no infinite descent of ever-finer structures exists below the Planck scale. The pattern converges -- the universe is finite in both directions.

name: Global Finiteness
summary: Global Finiteness: tau^3 is globally finite, structured, and closed. (1) Finite temporal extent: t_infty = sum p_k^{-1} < infty. (2) Finite spatial extent: T^2 compact, total volume finite. (3) Finite structural vocabulary: N_eternal motifs. (4) Convergent pattern: unique absorbing state.

name: What the chain does NOT prove
summary: The four-theorem chain proves structural finiteness (finitely many types of structure in a convergent pattern) but not quantitative finiteness (exact counts of galaxies, stars, or BHs). The chain establishes the universe is a finite book in a finite alphabet without determining the page count.

name: Defect entropy converges to zero
summary: Defect entropy converges as n -> infinity: lim S_def(n) = S_def^BH >= 0, the irreducible defect entropy from black hole excisions. If all BHs have merged into a single excision (by the Merger Normal Form), S_def^BH is the entropy of that final excision.

name: BH-dominated epoch
summary: The BH-dominated epoch begins at refinement depth n_BH where the BH contribution to S_def exceeds all other contributions combined: S_def^BH(n) > S_def^other(n) for all n >= n_BH. Stars have finite lifetimes; only BHs persist forever via the No-Shrink Theorem.

name: Coherence horizon
summary: The coherence horizon H_coh(n) at depth n is the diameter of the largest connected component of tau^3 minus the union of BH excision neighborhoods. As BHs grow and merge, excision neighborhoods expand and the coherence horizon shrinks, leaving a thin boundary layer between adjacent horizons.

name: Not a Big Crunch
summary: The shrinking coherence horizon resembles the Big Crunch but is structurally different: no recollapse to infinite density/curvature occurs. Excisions grow but remain well-defined topological features, the boundary layer stays finite, and no infinite density is reached. The endstate is a pattern, not a singularity.

name: Generative and refinement phases
summary: The generative phase (alpha_1 to alpha_{n_*}) is when new stable motifs are still created (particles, nuclei, atoms, stars, galaxies, BHs). The refinement phase (alpha_{n_*+1} onward) has no new motifs; existing ones are merely refined. The transition depth n_* is where N_stable reaches N_eternal.

name: Generative does not mean explosive
summary: The generative phase includes the Big Bang, inflationary, threshold-ladder, and conventional astrophysical epochs -- all are generative because new motif types appear. The refinement phase sees no new structure types, only mergers, cooling, and approach to the absorbing pattern.

name: Eternal Circulation Theorem
summary: The cosmological endstate is not heat death but eternal circulation: boundary characters chi_+, chi_- circulate continuously on L driven by the spectral gap lambda_1. Persistent BH excisions maintain non-zero defect entropy, preventing thermal equilibrium. The state is stable, non-equilibrium, and permanent.

name: Late-stage conditions favor complexity
summary: Late refinement-phase conditions (finite temperature, stable patterns, no disruptive motif creation) are precisely those favoring emergence and persistence of complex structures. The generative phase is turbulent; the refinement phase is stable and predictable -- an optimal habitat for life. Scope: conjectural.

name: Not the anthropic principle
summary: This is not the anthropic principle ("universe has these parameters because we observe it"). Tau says the progression operator drives the absorbing pattern toward a stable, warm, structured configuration that structurally attracts life. Life is a structural consequence, not a selection effect.

name: The key difference: no infinity
summary: Both heat death (infinite time) and Big Crunch (infinite density) involve infinities. The tau endstate involves none: proper time is finite, temperature bounded below, density bounded above, entropy bounded. Eternal circulation is a finite, stable, warm, structured steady state.

name: Scope note: CMB predictions C2, C5, C6
summary: CMB predictions C2, C5, C6 involve mapping tau-native quantities (sector couplings, inflation parameters) to standard CMB parametrization (Omega_b, n_s, r) through the calibration cascade, introducing residual uncertainties. Stated precisions (~2-5%) are estimates, not rigorous error bounds. Scope: conjectural.

name: Falsification levels
summary: Three falsification levels: Level 1 (structural) -- a sixth force, dark matter particle, c_gw != c, or GW echoes would falsify axioms K0-K6. Level 2 (quantitative) -- coupling constant deviations. Level 3 (scope) -- predictions within conjectural scope failing do not falsify the structural core.

name: The lesson: do not add, recognize
summary: The history of unification attempts suggests unification may require recognizing existing structure, not adding new structure. Maxwell unified E&M by recognizing one entity, not adding a fifth force. GUTs, strings, LQG each add structure (symmetry, dimensions, discreteness) creating new unexplained degrees of freedom.

name: Boundary Completeness
summary: All C(4,2) = 6 pairs of primitive sectors {D, A, B, C} satisfy commuting Hartogs squares in H_partial[omega]. Each pair has a well-defined cross-coupling as a graded-algebra morphism. The four forces are four projections of a single boundary holonomy algebra, not separate theories to be unified.

name: Comparison with orthodox unification
summary: The Boundary Unification Principle differs from orthodox unification in three ways: no larger gauge group (sectors are algebra projections, not Lie-group embeddings), no extra dimensions (tau^3 is 3-dimensional), and no new particles (no X/Y bosons, no superpartners, no KK towers).

name: Cross-coupling as naturality
summary: For each sector pair (X,Y), the cross-coupling kappa(X,Y) is the leading spectral weight of a natural transformation eta_XY between sector projection functors on H_partial[omega]. The naturality square commutes, ensuring universal coupling form independent of specific configurations.

name: Naturality replaces gauge invariance
summary: In orthodox gauge theory, universality of force couplings follows from gauge invariance of the Lagrangian. In tau, gauge invariance is replaced by naturality of cross-couplings as natural transformations. Naturality is stronger: it applies to the entire algebra of observables, not just the Lagrangian.

name: iota_tau mediates all ten couplings
summary: Every coupling constant in tau (self-couplings, cross-couplings, alpha, G, and the closing identity) is a rational function of the single parameter iota_tau = 2/(pi+e). No second free parameter exists. The unique fixed point of the progression operator determines all spectral weights.

name: Scope note: implementation roadmap
summary: The Boundary Completeness Theorem is tau-effective, but the computational implementation (mapping to SM observables like g-2, W/Z/Higgs masses, QCD running coupling) has not been carried out. The implementation roadmap is at the conjectural scope level -- an honest statement of what remains.

name: Part VI Achievement Summary
summary: Part VI established eleven structural results: Big Bang as regime, No-Singularity, Regime Invariance, Inflaton No-Go, Flatness from Compactness, BH Toroidal Topology, No-Shrink, Merger Normal Form, Global Finiteness, Eternal Circulation, and Boundary Completeness. Each stated with its theorem number and scope.

name: Open Questions
summary: Open questions after Part VI: the tau-sphaleron mechanism for baryogenesis, quantitative BBN predictions beyond leading order, the lithium problem, primordial gravitational wave spectrum, specific IMBH mass spectrum calibration, and the computational implementation of boundary unification.

name: Export to Books VI and VII
summary: Part VI exports to Book VI: the permanence hallmark, blueprint monoid, and eternal circulation as prerequisites for defining life. Exports to Book VII: the falsification pack, scope hierarchy, and boundary unification as inputs for the philosophical framework of reality.

name: Bridge to Part VII
summary: Part VI completed the physics of the universe from origin to fate. Part VII performs systematic comparison between the tau-framework and orthodox physics (GR, QFT, Standard Model, Lambda-CDM), identifying agreements, disagreements, and testable predictions at each interface.

name: Entries with ``No counterpart''
summary: Two tau-entities have no orthodox counterpart: the master constant iota_tau and the coherence kernel. Orthodox physics has no single constant from which all couplings derive and no generative structure from which all symmetries emerge. These are structural features that exist in tau but not in orthodox physics.

name: Preservation does not mean identity
summary: Preservation of empirical content does not mean tau and orthodox physics are the same theory. They differ structurally: tau has boundary algebra and zero free parameters; the Standard Model has 19 parameters and requires renormalization. The empirical content is shared; the structural content is not.

name: Structural artifact
summary: A structural artifact of a physical framework F is a problem, divergence, or paradox that arises within F but has no ontic counterpart in the boundary holonomy algebra H_partial[omega]. Seven orthodox artifacts (UV divergences, singularities, vacuum catastrophe, dark sectors, measurement problem, hierarchy problem, cosmological constant) are dissolved in tau.

name: The common thread
summary: All seven structural artifacts share a common origin: orthodox physics treats readouts (fields, metrics, wave functions) as ontologically primitive. When a readout develops pathological behavior, orthodox physics adds new structure to manage the pathology. In tau, the ontic level is the boundary algebra, and readouts are derived.

name: Ontic and readout layers
summary: The ontic layer is the boundary holonomy algebra H_partial[omega] and the enrichment functor E0 to E1; entities here are structural and observer-independent. The readout layer is the chart projection into coordinate-based observables. Orthodox physics operates at the readout layer; Category tau operates at and derives the readout from the ontic layer.

name: Orthodox physics is not wrong
summary: The ontological layer distinction does not imply orthodox physics is incorrect. Orthodox physics is chart-level: it works within a specific projection and computes correctly there. Errors arise only when chart-level entities (metric, wave function) are elevated to ontological status beyond their domain of validity.

name: Readout interpretation protocol
summary: Given an orthodox result R_orth, the readout interpretation protocol identifies its ontic source in H_partial[omega] (a boundary character, sector coupling, or spectral eigenvalue), checks the projection domain for validity, and verifies that the chart projection reproduces R_orth. This systematizes reading orthodox results as tau-readouts.

name: Where tau adds value
summary: For predictions like g-2, tau adds nothing to the numerical result since QED already computes it correctly. What tau adds is structural explanation: the perturbation series converges because the profinite tower is finite at every level, eliminating Dyson's factorial divergence argument. The series is exact, not asymptotic.

name: The vacuum catastrophe as diagnostic
summary: The vacuum catastrophe (10^120 discrepancy) is the clearest diagnostic of the ontological layer distinction. The manifold-based QFT sums over continuous modes at arbitrarily short wavelengths that the profinite boundary algebra does not support. The catastrophe is a counting error: summing degrees of freedom that do not exist.

name: The analogy of cartography
summary: Metaphorical analogy: orthodox physics is like map projections (Mercator, Peters, Lambert) covering different regions of a globe, each accurate within its domain but introducing distortions at edges. The artifacts are the distortions. Category tau is the globe itself, not a projection of it.

name: Properties of the correspondence functor
summary: The correspondence functor Phi from tau-observables (boundary characters on H_partial[omega]) to orthodox observables (Hermitian operators on Hilbert spaces) is faithful on the overlap domain, non-surjective (artifacts like UV divergences have no ontic source), and maps the five-sector decomposition to the gauge-group decomposition SU(3) x SU(2) x U(1) x Diff(M).

name: Non-surjectivity is a feature
summary: The non-surjectivity of Phi is the precise statement that structural artifacts (UV divergences, singularities, dark sectors) have no ontic source. They are elements of the orthodox observable category that do not lie in the image of the correspondence functor. Non-surjectivity is a feature, not a limitation.

name: Respect for the empirical record
summary: Any theory superseding QFT must reproduce its empirical predictions at the same precision. Category tau does so through the Empirical Preservation Principle: the correspondence functor Phi maps the refinement tower to the perturbation series, preserving every coefficient. The issue with QFT is not its predictions but its ontological commitments.

name: The microscope analogy
summary: Bulk ontology is like using a microscope below its resolution limit: the instrument produces images that are artifacts of the optics, not features of the specimen. QFT sees UV divergences, Landau poles, and vacuum catastrophes because it probes distances where its foundational assumptions (manifold, point interactions, infinite modes) are invalid.

name: Renormalization as tower truncation
summary: The renormalized QFT calculation at l loops with scheme S equals the readout of the boundary algebra H_l at refinement depth l under the chart projection determined by S: [QFT at l loops]_S = pr_S(H_l). Different renormalization schemes are different chart projections of the same ontic object H_l.

name: Scheme independence explained
summary: Scheme independence of physical observables is trivially explained: physical observables are ontic quantities in H_partial[omega], and different chart projections of the same ontic quantity yield the same physical value by definition. Scheme dependence of intermediate quantities reflects non-canonical chart choice, not physics ambiguity.

name: UV shield
summary: The UV shield is the structural property that every refinement level H_n of the boundary holonomy algebra is a finite-dimensional algebra. The projective limit H_partial[omega] = varprojlim H_n is profinite (compact, totally disconnected) and therefore admits no ultraviolet divergences. This is structural finiteness, not regularization.

name: Comparison with lattice QFT
summary: Lattice QFT replaces the continuum with a discrete lattice of spacing a, making loop integrals finite, then takes the continuum limit a to 0. The tau profinite tower resembles a lattice at each level but differs fundamentally: there is no continuum limit to take. The profinite completion replaces the continuum limit entirely.

name: Path integral recovery
summary: Under the chart projection with a Gaussian weight function, the boundary-character sum reduces to the Euclidean path integral Z_tau -> integral D phi_E exp(-S_E[phi_E]) in the continuum limit. The Minkowski path integral is obtained by Wick rotation (analytic continuation) of the readout. The path integral is a readout, not the fundamental object.

name: The Wick rotation is a chart operation
summary: In orthodox QFT, the Wick rotation t -> -i tau_E is a formal device with unclear physical justification. In tau, the Wick rotation is a chart operation: a change of projection from the Minkowski chart to the Euclidean chart. Both charts are readouts of the same boundary algebra H_partial[omega]. The ambiguity is resolved structurally.

name: The field as scaffolding
summary: The quantum field phi(x) is metaphorically scaffolding: erected during the calculation (perturbative expansion), supporting the structure as it rises, and removed when the building (physical prediction) is complete. The scaffolding is not part of the building. The quantum field is not part of the ontic structure.

name: Not all parameters have been computed
summary: Honesty requires stating that not all Standard Model parameters have been computed from tau. The electron mass is derived to 0.025 ppm, and W/Z/H masses to approximately 3%. Quark masses and CKM mixing angles have not yet been derived at comparable precision. The derivation is part of the ongoing research programme.

name: Profinite convergence
summary: The boundary-character expansion Z_tau(alpha) = sum c_n alpha^n converges absolutely for |alpha| < 1 (and for physical alpha approx 1/137). The n-th coefficient satisfies |c_n| <= C^n for a constant C determined by the coherence kernel. Dyson's factorial-growth argument does not apply because the profinite tower has finitely many orbits at each level.

name: Why m_n and not m_e?
summary: The neutron mass is chosen as the calibration anchor (not m_e, alpha, or G) because it is the most directly determined composite quantity in the tau ontic hierarchy. The neutron is the ground-state baryon, the simplest persistent defect bundle involving all three fiber sectors. The electron mass is then a prediction, not an input.

name: GR's scope
summary: GR is arguably the most successful single-equation theory in physics: G_mu_nu + Lambda g_mu_nu = (8 pi G / c^4) T_mu_nu accounts for all gravitational phenomena from laboratory scales to the Hubble radius. No other equation spans such a range. Any successor framework must reproduce this scope.

name: Spacetime in tau
summary: In Category tau, spacetime is not a substance or manifold. It is a readout: the chart projection of H_partial[omega] into a coordinate basis. The points of spacetime are addresses in the profinite tower, spectral labels (n, k_1, k_2) indexing boundary characters, not geometric points in a continuum.

name: GR as chart shadow of the tau-Einstein identity
summary: The Einstein field equation with Lambda = 0 is the chart shadow of the tau-Einstein identity: pr_chart(R^H = kappa_tau T) = G_mu_nu = (8 pi G / c^4) T_mu_nu. The chart shadow is valid in the regime where the profinite structure appears continuous (macroscopic scales). GR emerges as an effective readout, not a fundamental law.

name: The readout quantization obstruction
summary: Quantization constructs a boundary algebra from a classical phase space. If the classical object is already a readout of a boundary algebra, quantization produces a readout of a readout, a double projection that generates spurious degrees of freedom. This is why quantizing gravity (the GR metric) produces a non-renormalizable theory.

name: Gravity is already quantum
summary: In Category tau, gravity does not need to be quantized because it is already quantum. The D-sector of H_partial[omega] is a fully quantum structure with boundary characters, spectral decomposition, and commutation relations. The classical Einstein equation is the macroscopic limit, not the starting point.

name: No singularity theorem
summary: The tau-Einstein equation R^H = kappa_tau T admits no singular solutions. The holomorphic curvature R^H is an element of H_partial[omega], which is a compact (profinite) algebra. Compact algebras have no divergent elements. Therefore: no singularities inside tau-black holes, no Big Bang singularity in tau-cosmology.

name: The metric is a derived quantity
summary: In GR, the metric g_mu_nu is the fundamental dynamical variable. In tau, the metric is a secondary quantity: the chart shadow of the boundary algebra, computed from R^H by integrating the readout over a coordinate patch. As a derived quantity, the metric inherits its properties from the boundary algebra, not vice versa.

name: Sector Exhaustion --- no dark sector
summary: The five generators alpha, pi, gamma, eta, omega produce exactly five sectors through the Generator-Sector Correspondence. The coupling budget at every primorial depth is exactly exhausted. No sixth sector exists with nonzero coupling. Therefore dark matter (a sixth substance) and dark energy (a sixth force) do not exist within the tau framework.

name: Dualities as structural echoes
summary: From the tau perspective, string theory's dualities are structural echoes of the bipolar decomposition chi_+/chi_- on the lemniscate boundary. T-duality exchanges the two lobes of L; S-duality exchanges chi_+ and chi_-; mirror symmetry exchanges the two sectors of the Epstein zeta on the fiber T^2. Each duality captures one bipolar feature.

name: Landscape collapse
summary: The boundary holonomy algebra H_partial[omega] of tau^3 admits a unique ground state determined by the coherence kernel. The sector couplings, particle spectrum, and cosmological parameters are uniquely determined by iota_tau. There is no landscape: the vacuum is unique. This collapses string theory's 10^500 vacua to one mathematical constant.

name: Occam and dimensions
summary: The principle of parsimony favors tau over string theory: string theory postulates six unobserved extra dimensions introducing 10^500 vacua, while Category tau postulates no extra dimensions and produces a unique vacuum. The 3+1 dimensions emerge as chart projections: three spatial from T^2 plus the profinite radial direction, one temporal from tau^1.

name: Native holography
summary: The boundary holonomy algebra H_partial[omega] encodes the complete E1 physics of tau^3. The encoding is isomorphic (not approximate): there is a bijection between boundary characters on L and physical observables on tau^3. This is native holography, built into the tau^3 = tau^1 x_f T^2 fibered product structure, not postulated.

name: AdS/CFT as a partial echo
summary: AdS/CFT is a partial echo of tau holography. The duality captures the correct structural insight (bulk physics encoded on boundary) but implements it in a specific background (anti-de Sitter) that does not describe our universe. Tau holography is background-independent and applies to the actual (approximately de Sitter) universe.

name: iota_tau is a mathematical constant
summary: The number iota_tau = 2/(pi + e) involves only pi and e, two transcendental numbers defined independently of physics. It is not a vacuum expectation value, flux quantum, or modulus. It is a ratio of pure numbers, as fixed as pi itself. The contrast with string theory's 10^500 vacua versus one mathematical constant is maximal.

name: Why SUSY was not found at the LHC
summary: The LHC has excluded most natural SUSY scenarios below about 1 TeV. From the tau perspective, this is expected: SUSY is not a symmetry of the physical world. The bipolar symmetry chi_+/chi_- plays the structural role SUSY was designed to fill but does not predict superpartners. Their absence is a structural feature, not a problem.

name: The parable of the library
summary: String theory is metaphorically a library containing every possible book: it certainly contains the one describing our universe, but also every incorrect description. Without a catalogue identifying the right book, the library is useless. The landscape is this library. Category tau is a single volume whose content is determined by its axioms.

name: LQG's genuine contribution
summary: LQG's four achievements (background independence, area/volume quantization, BH entropy, cosmological bounce) represent forty years of serious non-perturbative quantum gravity research. Any successor framework owes intellectual honesty to explain what LQG got right and why these structural insights emerge as partial projections of the boundary algebra.

name: No knobs in tau
summary: The coherence kernel admits no continuous deformations (No Knobs Theorem, Book III). The sector couplings, master constant, and all derived physical quantities are uniquely determined. There is no analogue of the Immirzi parameter. This contrasts with LQG where the Immirzi parameter remains undetermined and must be fixed by hand.

name: The residual manifold in LQG
summary: LQG retains a residual manifold ontology (the embedding manifold Sigma) even though the smooth structure is not used in quantization. LQG's graphs and SU(2) spin labels are shadows of the manifold's rotation group. Category tau's profinite structure has no embedding manifold and is not a shadow of any manifold symmetry.

name: Four echoes, one architecture
summary: LQG and tau share four structural properties (background independence, discrete spectrum, area-law entropy, singularity avoidance) because both reject the fixed-manifold ontology. The difference: LQG retains residual manifold structure (graphs in Sigma, SU(2) labels) while tau completes the rejection through the algebraic-boundary framework.

name: CDT as a computational echo
summary: CDT can be understood as a computational exploration of the path integral that tau evaluates algebraically. CDT's causality condition is a computational proxy for the progression operator; the de Sitter geometry emerging from CDT simulations is a proxy for the FRW chart readout. CDT and tau agree qualitatively: causality first, smooth geometry emergent.

name: Sorkin's Lambda prediction
summary: Sorkin's prediction Lambda ~ 1/sqrt(N) deserves attention: it is the right order of magnitude and was made before observational discovery. From tau's perspective, the success is explained by coincidence between 1/sqrt(N) for Planck-volume cells in the observable universe and the Lambda-CDM fit. Tau predicts Lambda = 0 exactly, with acceleration as a regime effect.

name: Respect and replacement
summary: The structural comparison is not an attack on LQG, CDT, or causal-set communities. Their partial successes (background independence, discreteness, area-law entropy, bounce) are real insights that any successor must honor. Category tau explains why these insights are correct (they are projections of H_partial[omega]) and completes them structurally.

name: Twistor embedding
summary: The Penrose transform for massless fields on Minkowski space embeds into the E1 readout of the boundary holonomy algebra: H^1(PT, O(n)) embeds into the set of characters in H_partial[omega] with zero mass that factor through fiber sectors. Twistor theory captures the massless sector of the boundary algebra in the flat-space limit.

name: Twistors as shadow
summary: The twistor programme is the E1 shadow of the Central Theorem, projected onto the flat-space limit. Penrose's projective twistor space PT is the projective completion of the fiber spectral space restricted to the zero-mass sector. Twistors capture one sector projection; tau provides the full algebra including massive fields and gauge groups.

name: NCG spectral triple from tau
summary: The boundary holonomy algebra at E1 determines a canonical spectral triple (A_tau, H_tau, D_tau) where A_tau = O(tau^3) = A_spec(L) (from the Central Theorem), H_tau is the Hilbert space of square-integrable boundary characters, and D_tau = partial_L is the boundary Dirac operator. NCG's spectral triple is a natural readout of tau.

name: Gravity as readout --- no renormalization
summary: In the tau-framework, GR is a readout, not a fundamental theory. The tau-Einstein equation is a boundary-character identity, not a dynamical law on a manifold. Readouts do not need renormalization. The asymptotic safety programme's UV fixed point with two relevant directions corresponds to the two base-sector couplings kappa(D) and kappa(A).

name: The two relevant directions
summary: The most robust finding of asymptotic safety is that the UV fixed point has approximately two relevant directions. In Category tau, the base tau^1 has two generators: alpha (Gravity, D-sector) and pi (Weak, A-sector). The two relevant directions correspond to base-sector couplings kappa(D;1) and kappa(A;1) satisfying the temporal complement kappa(D)+kappa(A) = 1.

name: Verlinde's rotation curves
summary: Verlinde's 2016 rotation-curve prediction uses de Sitter entropy as the driving mechanism. Tau produces qualitatively similar modifications through boundary-curvature corrections to the Newtonian readout, but without invoking de Sitter entropy. The mechanisms differ; the predictions overlap. Discriminating observations are discussed in the falsifiable seams chapter.

name: Completeness of the boundary algebra
summary: Any sub-algebra P of H_partial[omega] that is closed under sector decomposition and contains at least one nontrivial character from each of the five sectors produces a consistent but incomplete physics. Orthodox programmes (QFT, GR, string theory, NCG, LQG, entropic gravity) are such sub-algebras. Tau is the minimal complete algebra containing all six.

name: No hierarchy among programmes
summary: The analysis does not rank the six orthodox programmes. Each captures a different projection of H_partial[omega]. The projections are complementary, not hierarchical. What is hierarchical is the relationship between projections and the full algebra: every projection is contained in H_partial[omega], and H_partial[omega] is contained in no projection.

name: VM representation of a quantum state
summary: A VM (virtual machine) quantum state is a vector |psi> in the orthodox Hilbert space obtained from a boundary character chi in H_partial[omega] by the readout map Read: chi -> |psi_chi>. The wave function is not a physical object; it is a VM representation of a boundary character. The VM language is orthodox QM; the ontology is the boundary algebra.

name: Measurement problem dissolution
summary: The measurement problem is dissolved: unitary evolution is the VM readout of character evolution under rho when no address resolution occurs (Read(rho^n chi) = U(n)|psi_chi>), and projection is the VM readout when address resolution occurs. Both are correct under different conditions. The Dirac-von Neumann conflict is a conflation of two valid projections.

name: Superposition in the VM
summary: A boundary character chi = chi_1 + chi_2 produces a VM state that looks like superposition |psi> = c_1|a_1> + c_2|a_2>. But chi_1 and chi_2 are components of a unique algebraic decomposition, not branches. The character is one object, not two parallel realities. Many Worlds mistakes the VM representation for the ontology.

name: Bell inequality in tau
summary: The CHSH inequality |S| <= 2 is violated in tau by exactly the quantum prediction |S| <= 2 sqrt(2). There are no hidden variables (boundary characters are the complete description) and no nonlocality (correlations arise from the joint character's algebraic structure). Bell correlations are pre-existing in the joint character, not communicated at measurement.

name: Entanglement as address sharing
summary: What orthodox QM calls entanglement is, in the tau-framework, address sharing: two subsystem characters that factor through the same sector component of the joint character. Address sharing is the algebraic analogue of two variables sharing a common factor. Spooky action at a distance is not action; it is revelation through address resolution.

name: Decoherence as address-resolution shadow
summary: Decoherence is the VM description of address resolution in the boundary algebra. The environment is the collection of boundary characters not in the system subalgebra; tracing over it projects the joint character onto the system's sector component. The diagonal density matrix is the VM representation of the resolved address, not a physical process of information loss.

name: The century of confusion
summary: The measurement problem has consumed enormous intellectual resources since 1927, devoted to resolving what is, from the tau perspective, a category error: confusing the VM representation (the wave function) with the ontology (the boundary character). The wave function is a map, not the territory. Collapse is not a physical process but a change of description.

name: No singularities in tau
summary: The tau-Einstein equation admits no singular solutions. The D-sector coupling kappa_tau = 1 - iota_tau is finite and nonzero at every refinement depth; the boundary character chi_D is bounded; the E1 curvature readout is bounded. Therefore: no singularities inside tau-black holes, no Big Bang singularity, no naked singularities.

name: No UV divergences in tau
summary: Every spectral sum over boundary characters in H_partial[omega] converges. For any sector X and depth N: sum ||chi_X(alpha_n)||^2 <= kappa(X)^2 N. Physical observables are obtained via profinite completion, not continuous integration. The limit exists because boundary characters are bounded. No regularization, renormalization, or cutoff is needed.

name: Renormalization is correct but unnecessary
summary: Renormalization is not wrong. It is a correct procedure for extracting finite physics from a divergent VM representation. What renormalization does is implicitly reverse-engineer the profinite finiteness of the boundary algebra within a manifold language that does not naturally express it. Renormalization is correct but unnecessary in the tau framework.

name: No dark sectors
summary: Dark matter does not exist: the five sectors exhaust the generator budget and no sixth sector has nonzero coupling. Dark energy does not exist: the vacuum energy of H_partial[omega] is exactly zero. The cosmological constant is zero. Observed phenomena attributed to dark matter/energy are readout artifacts of boundary-curvature corrections and regime effects.

name: Vacuum energy is exactly zero
summary: The tau-vacuum energy is rho_vac^tau = 0 (exact). The vacuum character chi_0 in H_partial[omega] has zero energy readout because: the profinite spectrum has no zero-point energy (each depth contributes a discrete character, not a continuous mode), and the ground-state character is uniquely determined by the coherence kernel with zero energy by construction.

name: The 10^120
summary: The 10^120 discrepancy is not a fine-tuning problem; it is a counting error. Manifold-based QFT counts continuous modes at arbitrarily short wavelengths that the profinite structure does not support. The predicted vacuum energy of 10^113 J/m^3 is the energy of modes that are not real. Only real modes (boundary characters) contribute, and their ground state has zero energy.

name: Why ``dissolve'' and not ``solve''
summary: The language of dissolution vs. solution is deliberate. A solution to the measurement problem would be a mechanism resolving tension within the Dirac-von Neumann formulation. A dissolution removes the formulation that produces the tension. The five hinges do not solve the hard problems; they dissolve them by replacing the framework in which the problems arise.

Every item has a clear path within tau. What is missing is computation, not structure. The structure is complete; the computation is ongoing.

summary: Two-Channel Acceleration Decomposition: on τ³ = τ¹ ×_f T², the total gravitational acceleration decomposes as g² = g_base² + g_fiber² = g_N² + g_N·a₀ (Pythagorean sum). Base channel (τ¹): g_base = g_N = GM/r². Fiber channel (T²): g_fiber = √(g_N·a₀). Equivalently g = g_N·√(1 + a₀/g_N).

name: Channel Fraction
summary: Channel Fraction: f_fiber = g_fiber²/g² = a₀/(g_N+a₀) = 1/(1+y) where y = g_N/a₀. Measures fiber contribution to total acceleration squared. NGC 3198 at 30 kpc: f_fiber ≈ 0.98 — fiber channel provides 98% of total gravitational acceleration.

name: Transition Radius
summary: Transition Radius: r_tr = √(GM/a₀), where g_N = a₀ (base = fiber channels equal). NGC 3198: r_tr ≈ 4.2 kpc ≈ 1.6 R_d. DDO 154: r_tr ≈ 0.25 kpc << R_d. Dwarfs and LSB galaxies have r_tr << R_d — entirely fiber-dominated.

name: Two-Channel Interpolation Theorem
summary: Two-Channel Interpolation Theorem: the decomposition g² = g_N² + g_N·a₀ defines ν_2ch(y) = √(1+1/y) with y = g_N/a₀. Newtonian limit (y>>1): ν→1, g→g_N. Deep regime (y<<1): ν→1/√y, g→√(g_N·a₀), giving v⁴ = GM·a₀ (BTFR). Both asymptotic regimes agree with standard μ_τ interpolation.

name: Algebraic Distinctness from Standard MOND
summary: Algebraic Distinctness: ν_2ch(y) = √(1+1/y) is algebraically distinct from standard Bekenstein ν_std(y) = √((1+√(1+4/y²))/2). Max difference +12% at y ≈ 1–2 (transition region). Both share identical deep-MOND and Newtonian asymptotics. Two-channel satisfies quadratic implicit equation g²−g_N²−g_N·a₀=0; standard satisfies quartic.

name: Lie Algebra Cross-Term
summary: Lie Algebra Cross-Term: ω_fiber = √(a₀/r) DERIVED from capacity gradient V.P56 + structural a₀ V.T207 (both τ-effective). For circular geodesics on τ³, g_cap = ω_fiber²·r gives ω_fiber = √(a₀/r) = c/√(2ℓ_τ·r). Cross-term: g_fiber = r·|ω_base|·|ω_fiber| = √(g_N·a₀). Uniqueness: only mass-independent angular velocity consistent with V.T85.

name: AQUAL as Projection

For spherical symmetry, AQUAL PDE ∇·[μ·∇Φ] = 4πGρ is algebraically equivalent. Nonlinearity appears upon projecting out fiber T². General non-spherical PDE form remains open.

Flat rotation curves are dominated by T² fiber. Keplerian 1/√r decline is the artifact of ignoring the fiber (classical regime y>>1).

name: Photon-Capacity Deflection

The capacity gradient deflects photons identically to massive particles because the τ-Einstein equation is a metric theory: photons follow null geodesics of the same effective metric g_∂[χ] that governs massive-particle geodesics.

summary: Each NF-prefix cylinder N_n(x) is clopen in tau^3. The cylinders nest: N_{n+1}(x) subset N_n(x), with intersection_{n>=1} N_n(x) = {x}. Proved via fiber structure of NF map over discrete NFRep_k.

name: Cumulative Topology Change
summary: N_BH(n) = count of independent non-contractible BH linking classes in H_1(T^2_n; Z) at refinement depth n. Non-decreasing by No-Shrink Theorem; mergers reduce count via linking-class composition but BHs cannot disappear.

name: Wilson-Loop Superstructure
summary: Graph S(n) whose vertices are BH excision neighborhoods and edges are Wilson loops connecting them. Cosmic web of filaments, walls, and voids is the chart-level readout of these linking relations.

name: r/R = iota_tau from Fiber Structure
summary: T^2 shape ratio r/R = iota_tau is tautological from the fibration: R = l_tau (fiducial length, gamma-generator), r = iota_tau * l_tau (eta-generator). QNM frequency ratio R/r = iota_tau^{-1} ~ 2.930.

name: Polarity Convergence via Contraction Mapping
summary: Polarity imbalance I_BH converges to unique fixed point I* = 1-2*iota_tau ~ 0.317 under affine contraction F(I) = (1-iota_tau)*I + iota_tau*(1-2*iota_tau) with slope kappa_D = 1-iota_tau < 1. Banach fixed-point theorem guarantees convergence.

name: omega-Germ Normalizer Norm_omega
summary: For an omega-germ sequence g = (g_n), the omega-germ normalizer is Norm_omega(g)_n := NF_n(g_n), applying the canonical normal form function at each stage n of the boundary character at the crossing point.

name: Idempotence of Norm_omega
summary: Norm_omega o Norm_omega = Norm_omega. At each stage n, NF_n maps into canonical representatives NFRep_n; applying NF_n to an element already in NFRep_n returns the same element.

name: Stability Index of omega-Germ
summary: For an omega-germ g, the stability index n_*(g) is the minimal stage at which Norm_omega(g) stabilizes under refinement. Beyond n_*(g), the germ has reached its final normal form.

name: Imbalance as Multiplicative Homomorphism
summary: The imbalance map Imb: g -> sigma(g)*g^{-1} (where sigma is the lobe swap) is a multiplicative homomorphism from normalized omega-germs to the blueprint monoid M_BH.

name: Basin Preservation under Ringdown Dynamics
summary: If two BH states x, y share NF_n(x) = NF_n(y) at depth n, then after ringdown (post-merger relaxation) they satisfy NF_m(x) = NF_m(y) for all m >= n. Ringdown does not split dynamical basins.

name: Transporter-Hartogs Commutativity
summary: For any pair of sectors (X,Y), the transporter morphism Tr_{X->Y}: H_partial^(X) -> H_partial^(Y) commutes with Hartogs restriction maps. Transporters are morphisms of filtered modules over the holonomy grading.

name: Carriers as Germ Images
summary: Every carrier type (fiber, base, crossing) is a functorial image of the germ functor: Carrier_X = pr_X o Germ_omega. Carrier assignments are natural in the sector variable.

name: Admissible Endomorphism Monoid HolEnd^adm_tau
summary: Sub-monoid of End(Germ_omega) preserving holonomy grading and sigma-involution. H_partial[omega] is isomorphic to HolEnd^adm_tau as graded monoids at E1. Admissibility is the tau-native analogue of gauge fixing.
